title: the Design Museum Shop
review_text: Wasn’t too bad, moderate selection of items to buy as souvenirs

title: the Design Museum Shop
review_text: The shop was beautifully done with the skate theme well worth the drop in

title: the Design Museum Shop
review_text: What a fascinating place. Thoroughly enjoyable and educational couple of hours. Shad Thames is a nice place to walk around too!

title: Royal College of Nursing Library and Museum
place_title: Royal College of Nursing Library and Museum
review_text: I’ve tried to visit many public libraries in London, and this one is in a great location. It’s a wonderful place to work quietly or read something. You can also access nursing thesis.

title: Royal College of Nursing Library and Museum
place_title: Royal College of Nursing Library and Museum
review_text:
Free to use library (you don't have to be a member to use it), with computers available for study and a wide range of nursing specific books and journals.

Upstairs has a quiet study area although unfortunately since of the ground floor cafe noises does filter upwards.  There are a also the occasional useful healthcare seminar hosted on site (check under events on the RCN website)

title: Royal College of Nursing Library and Museum
place_title: Royal College of Nursing Library and Museum
review_text: Changing exhibitions on the history of nursing.

title: Royal College of Nursing Library and Museum
place_title: Royal College of Nursing Library and Museum
review_text: Great place to work and study if you're a student nurse! Also, there's a nice quiet little park in which to sit and eat your in.

title: Royal College of Nursing Library and Museum
place_title: Royal College of Nursing Library and Museum
review_text: Assignment done, latest books for nursing and the  staff were all helpful. The place is very cozy to study. Ilove it

title: Royal College of Nursing Library and Museum
place_title: Royal College of Nursing Library and Museum
review_text: Tranquil environment to get some uni work done. Good books and the staff are very very helpful! Seems the only place I can really get anything done.

title: Royal College of Nursing Library and Museum
place_title: Royal College of Nursing Library and Museum
review_text: Well worth visiting the nursing exhibitions. Avoid the Oxford Street queues and have a coffee in the Library and Heritage Centre coffee shop.

title: Royal College of Nursing Library and Museum
place_title: Royal College of Nursing Library and Museum
review_text: Great for any nursing relates resources and information. Lovely little gem

title: Museum Street (Stop C)
review_text: It's literally just a bus stop

title: London Transport Museum Shop
review_text: A surprisingly cool collection of London souvenirs!  So may creative and interesting items for those looking for something to take home to remember your trip.  Though with mugs and keychains for each line it left me wondering, so people really have a favourite tube line or station? I did think being able to buy bags or visions with the upholstery fabric from the train cars to be an interesting sale item.  Definitely worth a visit when in the area.

title: London Transport Museum Shop
review_text: This place is just so great that I am fascinated with the vast variety of transport merchandise the moment I entered the public transport haven! Totally cant resist my temptation in bringing back home a piece of London Double Decker Bus plus some London tube souvenirs!

title: London Transport Museum Shop
review_text:
Positives:
Museum itself is awesome, exhibits are great and interactive elements are wonderful. Lots of very interesting info.
Negatives:
Queueing system was very confusing., sign said queue to the left for annual passes, we had bought an annual pass online so we waited on the left, but when we reached the desk we were told it was the wrong desk so we had to rejoin the back of the other queue and wait again. Once in it was really busy and there seemed to be barely any staff members around. We needed an accessible toilet but the only one we could find was closed. Cloakroom attendant told us there was one by the cafe kiosk on other side of ground floor, we went there and found this was incorrect. By this point my kid was starting to become quite stressed. We finally found a staff member who had to radio his manager to find out where there was a suitable toilet. It turned out to be upstairs in the cafe (luckily my kid does not have mobility issues), meaning that we had to leave the museum and queue up again to re-enter. By this time he had missed his slot to see Santa Claus! 😩
Overall a great museum but disappointing experience.

title: London Transport Museum Shop
review_text: A bit expensive but it's an annual pass so if youre a nerd then it's ideal for you

title: London Transport Museum Shop
review_text: The great collection of old vehicles and a wonderful inside into the history of transport in London which is really the life blood of the community here. I am very cynical about the yearly membership that you are required to buy to enter the museum which is £25. I'm thinking that most people who visit here would be from outside the UK so they will not get to use a yearly membership. This is just a blatant cash grab and very disappointing. It shouldn't be more than £10. The gift shop is also very expensive and these two things detracted from what would have been a fantastic museum.

title: London Transport Museum Shop
review_text: We visited the gift shop and came away with incredible and unique souvenirs of our trip.  We love the pillows made from the fabric from the tube chairs.

title: Hackney Museum
review_text:
I have visited this museum quite a few times, library on first floor. This museum is really good and my children always love it.
You can buy gifts from the shop and greeting cards.
Has good history About Hackney.
Customer service excellent and place is really clean.

title: Hackney Museum
review_text: Free entry museum with tons of history & information about Hackney. A lovely staff greeted me when I entered, and she told me there is an photography exhibition going on, the exhibition tell us stories about Hackney from 1970 till modern day by using different photography. Besides that, toilet is very clean too!

title: Hackney Museum
review_text: If you want to know more about Hackney, its people and its history, this is a place to visit (and it's free). Lots of useful information and items on display, it will tell you a lot about this area of London.

title: Hackney Museum
review_text: Small museum as a part of the Hackney community centre but definitely worth seeing, particularly to follow the development of Hackney over the past centuries as well as the social changes. It's free of charge and gives a better understanding of this area of London.

title: Hackney Museum
review_text: Second visit this weekend to attend the sewing workshop, The Threads that Bind Us, such a warm and friendly welcome for myself and two grandchildren, would definitely recommend this little museum in the heart of Hackney.

title: Hackney Museum
review_text:
I remember coming here back in 2012 and wanted to visit again in 2015. It has the history of Hackney in different categories, fully illustrated and always with something for kids to absorb.

I admire the enthusiasm of the organisers putting together different sections of how Hackney has changed. I had the feeling of being in a real Asian café in that part made to look like one.

title: Hackney Museum
review_text: This museum tells the history of hackney’s settlers, food, music, and homes. It ranges from quirky and fascinating (eel shops & 200,000 BCE digging tools) to enlightening and kid-friendly (many different groups of immigrants /where they came from, photographs from the past, interactive displays). Definitely give it a visit if you’re in love with Hackney or need somewhere to take the kids in the daytime. Bang on some steel drums.

title: Benugo at Young V & A
address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London, E2 9PA
review_text:
Cafe is positioned well within the museum, and it's kept fairly clean.

The coffee isn't good, though, and neither are the cakes. Everything is overpriced.
They serve on chipped plates.

title: Benugo at Young V & A
address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London, E2 9PA
review_text: Reading the reviews you would think this was the worst cafe ever. Not at all. Decent food selection with even children portion option (go find that). It is not a café I would go out of my way to come but it is not a bad option if you are here at the museum and need to fuel up yourself or your little ones. May be expensive but not a kidney sold at the black market amount. What do you expect? Its a cafe in the museum.

title: Benugo at Young V & A
address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London, E2 9PA
review_text: We found the staff rather rude - perhaps further customer service training needed? Poor selection and overpriced for the portion sizes. Bring your own!

title: Benugo at Young V & A
address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London, E2 9PA
review_text: Not much choice - hot options were unavailable and that was disappointing as the kids didn't really eat the sandwiches. This was not even that late in the afternoon!

title: Benugo at Young V & A
address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London, E2 9PA
review_text: Kids box meals are stale UPF sandwiches, the cooked food is expensive and stingy.

title: Benugo at Young V & A
address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London, E2 9PA
review_text: Small cafe, huge seating area. Good for a quick bite while at the museum.

title: The King's Gallery, Buckingham Palace
review_text: Such a beautiful gallery shop! Every corner is filled with unique artwork and handmade crafts. The staff were so friendly and happy to share the stories behind each piece. Perfect place to find a meaningful gift or just get inspired. Highly recommend visiting!

title: The King's Gallery, Buckingham Palace
review_text:
What a fabulous gallery wonderful insight into the Edwardian era.
Thank you also to Kira who gave an animated enlightening informative and passionate talk on Oscar Wilde.
Remember you can also revisit within a year with your ticket.

title: The King's Gallery, Buckingham Palace
review_text:
Nov25- what a fabulous little gallery. The curators were helpful and we had a semi-private tour with Little History of Sikhs on the Edwardians Style!
Enchanting gallery. Wonderfully surprised by Queen Alexandra and likewise the enthusiasm by the curator for her!
Thank you for a lovely morning

title: The King's Gallery, Buckingham Palace
review_text:
Can be busy but if you can shut out the madding crowds, the art (depending on the title of the works being show) is remarkable. We saw the Edwardian Gallery.
Timed ticketing - don't be late!

title: The King's Gallery, Buckingham Palace
review_text: Went for the Edwardian exhibition. Staff were really lovely, especially the young woman on the door. She was super friendly and helpful :) . The queue moved really quick and security was quick too, just a quick airport style bag scan. If you have large bags you can leave them in lockers. I didn't use the audio guide myself but lots of people did abs seemed to find it really useful. If you prefer to read like I do there are plenty of plaques around. Well organised and the collection was really well presented and grouped,  so well done to the team that put the exhibit together. Just be aware that it gets quite busy quickly as some gallery rooms are on the smaller side. Good for an hours visit and ofc the state rooms and the 3 parks/ gardens are very close by.

title: The King's Gallery, Buckingham Palace
review_text:
Elegant, well-organised, and full of treasures

Our visit to the King’s Gallery was part of the Royal Day Out ticket, which also included the Royal Mews and the State Rooms at Buckingham Palace — an excellent way to see three very different but equally fascinating parts of the Royal Collection.

Visitor flow through each section was well managed, avoiding that “crowd shuffle” feeling you sometimes get at popular attractions. There was ample time and space to appreciate the displays, and plenty to see at every turn.

The King’s Gallery itself houses some truly wonderful works of art. The curation felt thoughtful and balanced, with a mix of world-famous pieces and lesser-known gems. The included audio guide was particularly well done — concise but packed with insights, adding depth without overwhelming the visual experience.

If you’re interested in art, history, or simply want a calmer, more intimate part of the Royal Day Out experience, the King’s Gall

title: The King's Gallery, Buckingham Palace
review_text:
Lots and lots of different collections.
Loved the Vincent Van Gogh collection room 43.
“His work was also heavily influenced by nature and his own spiritual beliefs, leading him to explore subjects with reverence and to express his emotions, such as sadness or loneliness, through his art”.

They also had lots of collections inspired by the Bible, interpretations differ from artist to artist. Specially about the crucifixion of the Christ.

Didn’t take a lot of pictures because I was absorbed by the whole process.

The gallery is very very clean.
Great memories.

title: London Fire Brigade Museum
review_text:
I have mixed feeling about this pop-up museum and I was considering to provide only three stars; however, I am keeping in mind this is only a pop-up and temporary museum and once the redevelopment of Lambeth Fire Brigade completes, a proper and functioning Museum will be up and running.

The exhibition is very small and I'd recommend for people to go during an event or activity already planned; or just pop up to the Migration Museum (on the 1st floor) which I found it to be absolutely fantastic.

title: London Fire Brigade Museum
review_text: So little to see and all behind plastic walls. A handful of photos. Would not recommend in current state.

title: London Fire Brigade Museum
review_text: Visited the old museum which was excellent. I dont understand why opening the new one is taking so long to. It's been years in the pipeline!

title: London Fire Brigade Museum
review_text: Wouldn’t recommend to be honest. Really wasn’t much to look at apart from 2 fire engines and equipment sprawled all over the floor behind glass. The hands on part of making fire safety cards wasn’t good at all. There were hardly any pencils and no templates or card to draw on. My 2 daughters were disappointed as was I. Online it sounded like it would be great but it was such a let down. Especially because we had travelled from the other side of London just to visit.

title: London Fire Brigade Museum
review_text: Worth dropping in if you're just around the corner, but I travelled across London to see it and felt I'd wasted my time. There's a small history of the fire station just across the road (not the LFB in general!), but all the exhibits are bizarrely missing so you can just read the text. And the full contents of a fire engine are laid out on the floor, but without much interpretation. It would really be better if they didn't pretend this was a "museum" and waited till their permanent home was up and running.

title: London Fire Brigade Museum
review_text:
We drove nearly one hour to get there. We were very disappointed.

The museum is a couple of rooms: one with some pictures, the other one with a firetruck and its content.

In addition of this it is quite dirty and the toilet too.

The reason why of the two stars is because the staff at the entrance was at least quite friendly.

title: London Fire Brigade Museum
review_text: Beyond disappointed. Listed as open today 10-4pm. Locked doors. No update on google. Phone line doesn't connect. No info on the entrance. After investigating further online it's closed down. We travelled all the way in to Lambeth for our fire obsessed 3yr old's birthday. He's heartbroken in his fire fighting helmet. Angry. Doesn't even deserve one star

title: London Fire Brigade Museum
review_text: This was so disappointing travelled down from Liverpool to see the museum LFB has such an interesting history, other than pictures telling the history of their HQ and the war years. Fire brigade exhibits are2 appliances behind plastic walls with a load of equipment on the floor if you are thinking of visiting don't it's not worth the effort

title: The British Dental Museum
review_text:
Didn't even know this place existed but popped down after I attended a lecture here.

It was completely deserted, which is a shame as whilst small in size is high interesting in what it teaches you. If you're in the area definitely worth popping in for a few minutes.

title: The British Dental Museum
review_text:
It was interesting and informative.

I don't really know what constitutes a Museum; however, I thought the name was a bit misleading - as the space is very small and is basically a room joined to the library.

title: The British Dental Museum
review_text: Not quite a museum, more of a mildly diverting room full of teeth (real, porcelain and ivory) and some antique dental instruments. Unsurprisingly, given it's located in the British Dental Association building, the main focus is the life and work of the founder of the BDA. Although tiny, it might have earned a 4th star if there were slightly more focus on the experience of dental patients, not just practitioners, and if the room and exhibits were more ordered (the day I visited, some exhibits were out of place on top of the cabinets, obscuring the displays). That said, the staff were very obliging and nice enough to let me have a look around even though the museum is apparently usually only open on Tuesdays and Thursdays.

title: The British Dental Museum
review_text: Small but great museum. One of the best I have seen in London.

title: The British Dental Museum
review_text: This is the best museum in London.

title: The British Dental Museum
review_text: Very interesting

title: Art Fund
review_text: Love Art Fund, easy and all museums, galleries welcome the pass. Such a relief to visit a year round at -50% . Thank you so much and for the book of where to go and visit what as app is a little slow.

title: Art Fund
review_text: Helpful organisation, Scarlett explained what they do really well and I look forward to supporting more.

title: Art Fund
review_text: The first delivery of the Art Pass never came, so after a phone call a delivery of a new Pass was arranged with an additional month to compensate for the month that the missing card had prevented us from making use of. Once the second card finally arrived, the extended date was not on it, and the type of card was incorrect. An additional month was requested again to compensate another missing month of usability, but was then told no as I 'could always have used the email receipt'. Having checked my emails and all the FAQs and the Terms and Conditions it says the Art Pass is only usable with the card and nowhere does it say I could have done this. As a result, so much time has been wasted waiting for a card when apparently I could have been using the benefits all along. Feeling pretty mugged off.

title: Art Fund
review_text: Great pass but dreadful website. Slow, difficult to navigate and no option to search by map.

title: Art Fund

title: Art Fund

title: Art Fund

title: Art Fund
review_text: Great place

title: Great Court Restaurant
review_text: Our group of six visited the Great Court Restaurant for high tea. Very convenient location since we planned to visit the British Museum anyway. However, we were disappointed with the food. It looked pretty, but everything was a bit strange and unusual. The atmosphere was pleasant, but the service was lacking and there was no explanation of the items. We were also surprised to be charged for our two pitchers of water. I would look into other options the next time we’re in London.

title: Great Court Restaurant
review_text:
Lovely Afternoon Tea in a Stunning Setting

We enjoyed afternoon tea at The Great Court Restaurant inside the British Museum, and it was a fantastic experience. It’s a brilliant idea — you get to walk through one of the world’s most iconic museums (entrance is free), and then relax under the beautiful glass roof with a classic afternoon tea.

The setting is elegant but not stuffy, and the afternoon tea was both tasty and reasonably priced by London standards. The scones were warm and fresh, the pastries were delicate and well-crafted, and the tea selection was excellent.

It’s a great way to combine culture and a little indulgence without breaking the bank. Highly recommended for visitors looking for a memorable and relaxing break in the heart of the museum.

title: Great Court Restaurant
review_text:
What I originally thought would be the highlight of our London trip — the Afternoon Tea experience at the Great Court Restaurant — turned out to be a major disappointment. Given its location in one of the world’s most prestigious museums, our expectations were understandably high, but the reality fell far short.

For £38 per person, this afternoon tea offers no sense of luxury at all. There was no warm greeting, no loose-leaf tea (the tea was served in bags), and no attention to detail. We were seated for quite some time before any staff approached us, and when they did, it was clear they had no record of our online preorder.

The staff member who served us appeared rushed and impatient. I felt something was off — we were not treated with the same care as other guests. I noticed that staff introduced the menu to other tables, checked in on how their food was, and even offered to take photos for them. In contrast, when our afternoon tea was served, there was no explanation at all, and afterwards no follow-up, no service, no questions.

As we were about to leave, the staff member suddenly came over. I initially thought she was going to ask about our experience, but instead she bluntly asked whether we had paid the service charge when we prepaid online. I said I didn’t remember and felt confused. She then took us to the counter, printed out the bill, and asked us to pay the service fee. My confusion was not about the fee itself, but about what service I was actually paying for — aside from the venue being inside the British Museum.

I paid in the end simply because I didn’t want to make a fuss. Perhaps the restaurant assumes tourists will only come once. The food itself was acceptable: the sandwiches had some interesting flavour combinations, while the desserts were a bit sweet for my taste. Overall, however, the entire experience left me feeling dissatisfied and treated differently.

title: Great Court Restaurant
review_text: I never have great expectations for a museum restaurant, so were pleasantly surprised by our wonderful experience at the Great Court Restaurant. We did book a table ahead of time, which I would highly recommend.  We requested a quiet table to the side which the staff gladly accommodated. All in all attentive service throughout the meal.  We pre-ordered a lunch special with two courses per person, so were able to try out many dishes, and are happy to report that food was surprisingly good.  Beyond what we expected. Excellent quality ingredients. Perfectly executed preparations. French bistro inspired dishes. I’d be hard pressed to pick a favorite., so encourage everyone to explore the menu.  In an institution so rich in cultural wonders, this meal made our visit that much more special.

title: Great Court Restaurant
review_text: Food is eatable but very so so here. We waited over an hour for our main course……one great thing about the restaurant is that you can prebook your meal online through British museum website and it makes it cheaper and you can reserve your seats on the website which guarantees you have somewhere you can sit down and take a rest from the visit of the museum.

title: Great Court Restaurant
review_text:
We stopped here half way through our visit to the Museum.
We had the Afternoon Tea, which was just perfect. Traditionally presented, delicious finger sandwiches, the scones were warm & the petite cakes topped everything off beautifully.
The service was excellent, welcoming & friendly & we didn't feel weird asking to share the Tea.

title: Great Court Restaurant
review_text: The restaurant is a delightful place to enjoy a break during your visit. The setting is bright and elegant, with beautiful views of the Great Court. The food is fresh, flavorful, and beautifully presented, and the menu offers great options for all tastes. Staff are warm, attentive, and genuinely welcoming. Prices are fair for the quality and the unique location. Overall, it’s an excellent choice that adds even more pleasure to the museum experience.

title: London Transport Museum Cafe
review_text: Overall good experience, not a fancy manu, but you can eat a good food at decent price. You can enjoy benches that feels like you are at station, design and seat cover reminds of you of London underground.

title: London Transport Museum Cafe
review_text: Called in for a little something to eat pre theatre. Chose sausage butty and tea....£7.50 and that's exactly what I got. Pretty basic, not  a complaint, as it's a sausage butty at the end of the day!  2 sausages in a buttered bap and a pot of tea. Service was good.

title: London Transport Museum Cafe
review_text: Very kind personnel, but coffee was not good, scone as well. Atmosphere very low as the museum is very crowded and the cafe is not immune to the rush below

title: London Transport Museum Cafe
review_text:
Nothing savoury that I could eat as a vegan, all they could offer me was carrot cake.

But the staff were fantastic, really friendly and tried their best to be accommodating.

title: London Transport Museum Cafe
review_text: London Transport Museum Canteen is a lovely place with unique style and friendly staff. Kid and family friendly. It provides toilet facilities. Cool fact: this place named after the static and mobile canteens that used to cater for London Transport workers during the 1940s and 1950s and celebrates London Transport's design legacy with moquette seats, posters and maps plus real tiles, seats and signs reclaimed from the Underground. Canteen is open to the public as well as Museum visitors for breakfast, lunch and dinner as well as snacks, pre-theatre dining, nibbles or just drinks at the bar. I had a very nice hot chocolate that I heartly recommend! Good to know: you can only leave tip in cash at the bar's tip jar.

title: London Transport Museum Cafe
review_text:
Disappointing Experience

I was surprised to find no coffee available just five minutes before 5 PM—odd for a place of this caliber. The luggage seemed outdated and overpriced, and the cabatte wasn’t impressive either. Staff came off as quite rude—perhaps tired, but that’s no excuse for poor service.

The biggest issue, however, was discovering mice under the table. Completely unacceptable. The manager is apparently aware of the problem, but nothing seems to have been done about it. That alone makes this a place I won’t be returning to.

title: London Transport Museum Cafe
review_text:
The white chocolate and raspberry muffins were disappointing and we exchanged them for scones, which were not fresh.  Reheated to make them edible.
All the cakes were left out, uncovered,  on a shelf

Been coming here for years, but this is our last visit.

title: London Transport Museum Cafe
review_text: Horrible experience. Just after paying £70, the girl in charge of the cafe lied to me, telling me that I couldn't go to the customer toilet because they were closing. They close at 6pm and it was 5.30pm. The older woman behind me begged her to go, saying she needed to go to the toilet and she wouldn't let her either. But she did let her friend in two minutes later while the rest of us were walking down the stairs. Inhumane, really. Don't spend money in a place like this with staff like this.

title: Science Museum Shake Bar by Benugo

title: Science Museum Shake Bar by Benugo

place_title: Science Museum Shake Bar by Benugo

title: Museum Dental Suites
review_text: After visiting multiple clinics I ended up choosing this one. I live in Bristol but travel all the way to London for my aligners and check up appointments. Dr Wahab has been such a help. Very transparent and informative which reassures me that I made the right choice. He cares about the details too and does everything with the patients best interest at heart. I’m really happy with my results so far. Thank you to the team for all their hard work.

title: Museum Dental Suites
review_text:
I am a very anxious patient when it comes to oral health, and I honestly couldn’t recommend this practice enough. Fabrizio has been my dentist for a few years now and he is amazing — extremely knowledgeable, calm, and genuinely able to put me at ease. He always takes the time to explain things clearly and never makes me feel rushed or judged.
The whole practice offers a very high standard of care: friendly staff, clean and professional environment, and a really reassuring atmosphere overall. I feel very well looked after here.
Highly recommended!!!

title: Museum Dental Suites
review_text: To say I was anxious about going to the dentist is an understatement. But Dr. Fabrizio Castellari has been very kind and patient with me throughout the treatment, explaining everything—what was happening at each step and what I should do afterward. I had to have an extraction, and I couldn’t believe I did not feel it at all. I would highly recommend Museum Dental Suites to anyone, especially those who are nervous. From my first call to reception until after my treatment, the whole team has been very kind, knowledgeable and efficient, making the experience as comfortable as it can be.

title: Museum Dental Suites
review_text: Dr. Wahab is a true star in dentistry! He fixed my teeth perfectly, and he's also a very nice person. The wonderful and helpful assistants, especially Kasia, deserve special praise. I highly recommend this dental practice to everyone.

title: Museum Dental Suites
review_text: I can’t praise Museum of Dental Suites enough. The service I got for my dental implant was perfect. Dr. Sam Parsno is someone you can 100% trust and feel safe with. Thank you so much. I am forever grateful.

title: Museum Dental Suites
review_text: Needed an emergency temporary filling put in and Dr. Wahab sorted me out perfectly while being transparent about costs. Great dentist who clearly cares about his patients and the work he does. The staff seem to genuinely enjoy working there as well. Good vibes

title: Museum Dental Suites

title: St James's Park
review_text:
St James's Park feels gentle, open and quietly ceremonial at the same time.
I enjoy walking through it without haste, following the paths around the lake, watching birds, pelicans and people sharing the space naturally. There is always movement, yet it never feels rushed. The park has a softness to it — water, trees, flowers and sky working together in calm balance.
What makes St James’s Park special for me is its setting. With views towards Buckingham Palace on one side and Whitehall on the other, it carries a subtle sense of history and state, yet remains entirely accessible and human. Benches invite you to sit and observe, not just the landscape, but London itself.
For me, St James’s Park is a place of quiet continuity — elegant without being distant, lively without being loud, and always reassuring in its calm presence.

title: St James's Park
review_text: A beautiful morning exploring the park today. Lots of spring flowers in bloom! So nice to be able to walk through in the middle of London.

title: St James's Park
review_text: Absolutely beautiful park. You can see the London Eye and Big Ben from here, and it’s very close to Buckingham Palace. There are lots of squirrels, a wonderful lake, and many ducks, swans, and pelicans. A perfect place for a relaxing walk.

title: St James's Park
review_text:
A masterclass in urban tranquility, St Jame's Park is nestled between iconic landmarks, and it offers a lush, immersive landscape that feels worlds away from the city’s bustle.

I adored the lake, with its elegant pelicans and swans, and mirrored reflections. If time permitted, I could sit and watch them  the whole day! Another lovely sight was the distant silhouette of the London Eye amidst the lush greenery.

The park’s layout invited slow wandering: deck chairs scattered across sunlit lawns, winding paths shaded by trees and quiet corners where wildflowers bloom. The park is simply a place where nature, history and city life converge with grace.

title: St James's Park
review_text: The perfect park in Central London to appreciate the joy and splendour of autumnal colours at their finest. The Blue Bridge spanning St. James’s Park Lake, offers a stunning partial backdrop of The London Eye, Whitehall and the City’s Towers. On a clear blue sky Sunday afternoon in October, the lake was stunningly blue with a near perfect reflection 💯in places.

title: St James's Park
review_text: St. James’s Park is absolutely beautiful! The views of Buckingham Palace and the lake are stunning. It’s such a peaceful place to walk, relax, and take pictures. We saw lots of ducks, swans, and even some friendly squirrels. There are plenty of benches and a little café for coffee or snacks. It’s the perfect spot to unwind and enjoy nature right in the heart of London.

title: St James's Park
review_text: St James’s Park is a peaceful oasis in the middle of London. Walking along the paths, watching the swans and ducks, and enjoying the greenery felt incredibly relaxing. The views of Buckingham Palace and the surrounding historic buildings add charm. A perfect spot to unwind and soak up the city.

title: St James's Park
review_text:
A nice and well looked after park.
It was a big pleasure to see the great white pelican knowing that it breeds from southeastern Europe through Asia and Africa only

title: The Regent's Park
review_text:
Regent’s Park is one of the most beautiful green spaces in London. Regardless this season of leaves falling down . The park is very clean, well organized, and perfect for walking, relaxing, or spending time with family. I especially enjoyed the gardens and the peaceful atmosphere.

Highly recommended for visitors of all ages 🌿

title: The Regent's Park
review_text:
Regent's Park is situated next to the underground station of the same name, which is on the Bakerloo line. Designed by John Nash in the early 19th century, the park is part of the Regent’s Park Estate and covers approximately 395 acres.

The park is divided into several sections, including the Inner Circle, the Outer Circle, Primrose Hill, and the Regent's Canal. It also features various attractions such as the London Zoo, the Open Air Theatre, the Rose Garden, boating on the lake, sports facilities, and the Regent's Canal. Most areas of the park, including the gardens, lakes, and sports sections, are free to visit. However, entry to the London Zoo and certain events at the Open Air Theatre require tickets.

Regent's Park is very picturesque, with vast open spaces that are perfect for picnics and walks. Although navigating the park can be confusing, it is advisable to stay near the entrances or the main central path. There is a café available, though it can be quite expensive. Free toilets are also provided. The park is an ideal spot for photographers and walkers.

Its close proximity to many attractions and a 15-minute walk from Oxford Street make it a great destination for tourists. Regent's Park is an iconic location that is always worth a visit.

title: The Regent's Park
review_text: The park was beautiful. It was a cold November day, below zero, but the walk was absolutely worth it. Squirrels were running around and there were plenty of birds everywhere. I promised myself I will come back in the summer to see this beautiful place in warmer weather.

title: The Regent's Park
review_text: Regent’s Park is a beautiful and spacious park in central London, perfect for a relaxing walk, a picnic, or just enjoying nature. The rose gardens are stunning in bloom, and the wide open green spaces make it feel peaceful even on busy days. There are also plenty of benches, a lake with ducks, and views that change beautifully with the seasons. Whether you're going for a jog, meeting friends, or just clearing your head, it's a lovely escape from the city buzz without leaving London. A true gem!

title: The Regent's Park
review_text: Regent’s Park in London is one of the most beautiful royal parks, covering about 395 acres. The park has around six main entry gates, including Chester Gate, York Gate, and Clarence Gate, making it easy to access from different sides. It features stunning rose gardens, wide walking paths, a boating lake, children’s play areas, and the famous London Zoo. A perfect place for walking, picnics, and enjoying nature in the city.

title: The Regent's Park
review_text: Regent’s Park is such a peaceful escape in the middle of London. The gardens are stunning, the lake is calm, and the whole place just feels refreshing. Perfect for a walk, a quiet coffee, or simply to clear your head ,it never disappoints.

title: The Regent's Park
review_text: Regent's Park truly is a London gem, offering a perfect escape right in the city center. It is wonderfully peaceful, making it easy to relax and unwind. The park facilities are excellent and well-maintained, but the real showstopper is the current scenery: the autumn colors are spectacular, creating a dazzling canopy of reds, golds, and oranges. Best of all, it never feels overly crowded, ensuring you always have space to enjoy the beauty. A must-visit park for tranquility and natural splendor!

title: The Regent's Park
review_text:
Regent's Park feels expansive, elegant and thoughtfully composed.
I enjoy walking here for its sense of order and variety. Broad paths open into quieter corners, formal gardens transition into open lawns, and the park always seems to offer a new perspective with each visit. The balance between structure and nature is particularly satisfying.
The gardens — especially the rose garden — add colour and refinement, while the water, wildlife and open space keep the park grounded and alive. Even when it is busy, Regent’s Park never feels crowded.
For me, Regent’s Park is a place to walk with intention — spacious, composed and quietly uplifting.

title: Battersea Park
review_text: Battersea Park is a true gem in London—spacious, peaceful, and full of variety. Whether you're into jogging, cycling, or just a relaxed stroll, the park offers beautifully maintained paths, a serene lake with boats, and plenty of benches to unwind. There’s also a small zoo, a charming café, and lovely gardens that change with the seasons. It’s a perfect escape from the city buzz without actually leaving the city. Great for families, solo visitors, or a laid-back date. Always something calming about this spot.

title: Battersea Park
review_text: Battersea Park was a relatively a normal park and really nice for walks. It felt like a normal, well-used park with lots of kids playing, a playground, and a fitness area. There were also some small statues and memorials around, which added a bit of character. I noticed there’s a gallery and a café too, although the gallery was closed when I went. Overall, a solid spot for a relaxed walk or family visit.

title: Battersea Park
review_text: It's a great park, my dog very much enjoyed going for a stroll there. Didn't expect to see a giant Buddhist pagoda, but it was pretty cool. Seems like you can buy Christmas trees there too, didn't expect that either.

title: Battersea Park
review_text: Battersea Park is truly a gem in London. Large green space, wooded areas, plua a beautiful lake with boating. Sport facilities Including tennis courts and playground. Plenty of walking paths. The Peace Pagoda is a tranquil spot. The whole park is well maintained and tidy. Recommend a visit

title: Battersea Park
review_text:
Battersea Park is honestly one of my favourite spots in London. It’s such a nice escape from the busy streets, and the wildlife makes it feel really special – you’ll see swans, ducks, and loads of squirrels running about. The lake is beautiful and really peaceful, especially if you just want to sit and watch the world go by for a bit. It never feels too crowded either, which I love.

The park is also perfect for a chilled day out. There are plenty of picnic areas where you can spread out a blanket and relax, and the old Pump House is a cool little historic touch that gives the place some character. The walking paths are lovely – you can do a long loop around the park or just wander through the tree-lined areas. It’s the kind of place where you can go for a jog, a dog walk, or just a lazy stroll and it always feels refreshing.

Food-wise, there are a couple of cafés and spots where you can grab something to eat or a coffee, which makes it easy to spend a whole afternoon there. Between the wildlife, the lakes, the space to relax, and the fact you can get food without leaving the park, it really has everything you need for a day out. Definitely a place I keep going back to.

title: Battersea Park
review_text:
The park is great and has a lot of attractions within itself. The Buddhist temple, lake, zoo. It was great to visit this place in the night for a shine walk for cancer research UK.

I would recommend this place for 10k and other long distance trainings.

title: Queen's Park
address: Queens Park Lodge, Harvist Rd, London, NW6 6SG
review_text:
Closes too early and opens too late.

On the 9th of October they close at 18:00-when it’s still light outside.

In the height of the summer, when we had a heatwave, it was already hot and sunny at 7:30-beautiful morning to excercise in the park and walk the dog before +30c heat, but the park was closed. But in October it opens at 7am. Make it make sense! What’s the point of the green space that cannot be used within reasonable hours? With these hours,it’s more of a fancy private garden than a public park.

title: Queen's Park
address: Queens Park Lodge, Harvist Rd, London, NW6 6SG
review_text: Interesting park with varying recreational facilities. For adults there's a fun putting green.  Children have two play parks, both  of which feature wooden pole swing stands, climbing frames, picnic benches, and area dividing fences also provide respite seating. The kids also have a sloping edge paddling pool which l assume will be filled with water when it's warmer. There's a nice looking cafe l didn't queue by because l had brought food with me. My companions who did go for drinks complained that th sign saying cards only was far too small so they came back empty handed and very disgruntled. Please vendors make your signage large enough for seniors and families to be able to read from 3 or 4 yards away. Plenty of mature trees provide shade for picnics. At th moment there is perhaps less colour to enjoy than in more manicured parks in boroughs like Westminster where more money is spent on shrubbery. The daffodil field though promises to be epic when all the blooms open. It's been a bit too cold for many plants to emerge.

There are also tennis courts and a basketball court. The park is very well maintained with planted flowerbeds adding much colour to the area. Most of the pathways areas are basically lit at night time making is possible to walk through it at night but I would use caution, only doing so accompanied by at least one other person for safety.

Though compact, it offers a refreshing mix of high-quality landscaping and a natural riverside feel. The park sits between the New River and the West Reservoir, making it an ideal spot for a calm walk or a quiet sit-down with a view of the water.
Some parts are currently closed off for renovation until January 2026, but there’s still enough open space to enjoy a leisurely stroll.

title: Millfields Park

review_text: Loved this playground. Has small swings and 2 big circle swings, a football/basketball court and a toddler play area with 2 in ground trampolines

title: Marcus Garvey Park
review_text: We love walking here with our family! Everything is always clean and well-maintained!

title: Marcus Garvey Park
review_text: Nice play park but could use some TLC. There was broken glass and rubbish on the grassy area.

title: Marcus Garvey Park
review_text: Basic park with over grown shrubs. Zero cleaning with a lot of cigarette stubs everywhere. If it were clean would be a very nice park. But right now just a small stroll and nothing much. Wont go there except for smoking if i were a smoker.

title: Marcus Garvey Park
review_text: Nice Park with a focus on kids play. But sometimes you will find basketball players blasting music with a lot of swearing. Not good for the kids and I'm assuming annoys the neighbours. Doesn't bother me directly though.

title: Claremont Park
address: Claremont Way, Brent Cross, London, NW2 1AJ
review_text: Such a lovely little find! We actually drove past and had to turn back once we spotted it — and I’m so glad we did. The decor is beautiful, the atmosphere is calm and welcoming, and it’s such a picturesque spot. The coffee and staff were amazing too. There’s limited parking outside (around 20 minutes), which is the only downside, as it’s definitely somewhere you’d want to stay longer. Will absolutely be back!

title: Claremont Park
address: Claremont Way, Brent Cross, London, NW2 1AJ
review_text:
I was with my daughter in this park on Tuesday afternoon. It wasn't busy. The place is very nice, lots of greenery and flowers which make it look like a meadow. There is a small lake with beautiful water lilies and many benches along the path. The playground is very big, clean and well organized. There are water pumps, sandpits, swings, slides and obstacle courses. My daughter was very happy. There is a cafe and toilets by the playground. Parking is available in front of the park.
The only downside is the noise coming from the building site which is right next to the park. But for children who like to look at construction machinery, especially big cranes, it will be a lot of fun.
Next to this park, there is another playground, as nice as this one, but without the water pumps.
I highly recommend visiting this park with children.

title: Claremont Park

The security always keep and eyes look after the dogs owner. Make them feel uncomfortable.and I like bring my dog sitting the bench near  the pond just relax .one guy came（ without uniform) say hey. Put the lead  on..why..we sitting in the bench do nothing.because I will put the lead on when  we leave.  So I just carry  my dog leave..I won't come  again .make me uncomfortable and not happy

title: Claremont Park

review_text: Very picturesque park and well maintained. The water play looks really nice. There are a few things for little children but most things are for juniors. I really like the wild flowers next to the pavement.

title: Claremont Park
address: Claremont Way, Brent Cross, London, NW2 1AJ
review_text: An excellent new park for all the family to enjoy. Free Summer activities, multi-sports on Fridays during the Summer holidays. Our kids 12, 8, 5 and 2.5 really enjoyed it. Water and sand to play with, lots of challenging equipment,a beautiful pond. We loved it.

title: Claremont Park
address: Claremont Way, Brent Cross, London, NW2 1AJ
review_text: Had a great few hours with some older kids as we came here after a session at flipout and wanted to see what it's like. It has two parks one for smaller children but older kids can use it too, it's also good for children with additional needs as it's accessible. The park has a lovely huge pond as you walk through to otherside and some really good play equipment that's a little more challenging, also has water to play with but wasn't on at the time we went. Very clean area. Has a ice cream and snake bar and toilets within the park.

title: Claremont Park
address: Claremont Way, Brent Cross, London, NW2 1AJ
review_text: Beautiful landscaping and naturalistic planting.  Well managed and safe.  Play space laid out to allow free and explorative play from toddlers to teens and a nice cafe and small shops where you can park.

title: Victoria Park Chinese Pagoda
review_text:
The Chinese Pagoda is a significant landmark in Victoria Park, located on a peaceful island within an ornamental lake, creating an oasis of calm.

Visitors can access the pagoda via two bridges, making it a serene spot to relax while enjoying beautiful views of the surrounding park and lake.

The park is conveniently located near public transportation and is a lovely destination for picnics or simply unwinding on a sunny day.

title: Victoria Park Chinese Pagoda
review_text: Absolutely beautiful spot inside Victoria Park. The Chinese Pagoda looks stunning, especially in the early morning or just before sunset. It's located on a small island in the middle of the lake, connected by a lovely bridge. Very peaceful and a great place to take pictures or just sit and relax for a bit. One of the hidden gems of East London.

title: Victoria Park Chinese Pagoda
review_text: Beautiful vibrant park. It has got everything you need. Seating, lake, food and drinks and toilets. Tourists tip: The pagoda is a real landmark, do not miss it!

title: Victoria Park Chinese Pagoda
review_text: Lovely park not to far from Stradfird Station (1km). Relaxing and nice for walk . Coffee shops near lake and children's playground and skate board area. A Gem of a place. Get your daily  steps up!

title: Victoria Park Chinese Pagoda
review_text: Very nice, they have just cut back the overgrown shrubbery. Lovely view of the lake.

title: Victoria Park Chinese Pagoda
review_text: Very quick stroll through here on our last day in London before we headed to the airport.  It was a lovely way to end our 10 day trip.  I can see why my cousin loves to visit this park and spend the day here.  Just beautiful!

title: Victoria Park Chinese Pagoda
review_text:
The original Victorian pagoda was demolished in the 1950s and was restored in 2011. A new bridge was built to architect James Pennethorne's original designs.
The island sits in the West Boating Lake. It  can be easily accessed from Crown Gate West, Royal Gate West, and Bonner Gate.

title: Victoria Park Chinese Pagoda
review_text:
...a very beautiful park, I always love visiting any time I'm in London..a good place to take a walk and relax.
It's absolutely lovely!

Once parked they entrapped my car in this place as I parked Friday and was slightly late after closing time. On google picture and their banner states it’s open on a weekend 8:30am to 6:30pm. However, it’s CLOSED on weekends!!! They charge price based on how they feel. Says £6 all day. They kept my over the weekend and Monday bank holiday. They tried to charge me £132. I had to argue my way out as they have price in their office saying parking for a week cost £70. Came to an agreement of a week’s worth. When the manager came, he tried to charge me £75 not stated anywhere in the place. Tried to say, it’s their new price when on the poster infront of my face said its £70 for a week. AVOID AT ALL COST! title: Regency Car Park - Swiss Cottage
review_text: We spent a weekend in central London and wanted to avoid the daily congestion charge - I was recommended this place.  The place itself was REALLY easy to find, well signed etc.  It does exactly as advertised and was resonably priced considering other options.  The only reason I am giving it four stars and not five is because it is advertised as a patrolled car park but we did not see anyone close by and the barriers were always raised on both sides.  It seems safe enough but, as I said, I didn't see anyone patrolling at any point.  Our car was there from Friday to Sunday and we had no issues though!  I would recommend this place.

title: Babylon Park London
review_text:
Brilliant place!
Rides, arcades and VR experience. We had the paid tickets for 2hours unlimited access to all rides and arcades. Well worth it - just scan and enjoy.

Great for adults, kids, teenagers. Plenty of things to do. Does get busy at weekends but that’s expected as the offer free entry but then you just pay for what you want. My child enjoyed it and overall was a great day out.

They have buggy parks on each floor so that’s handy and also toilets too. Food available there but just basic, but the location is near a lot of food shops / markets.

Will be going back for sure! ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

title: Babylon Park London
review_text:
Took the kids (ages 15, 14, 11, 9 and 6) and they absolutely loved it! Definitely better than other arcades — you get way more for your money. We paid £30 per child for 90 minutes of unlimited rides and games, which was great value.

It’s doable to get through most of it, but with younger kids (especially if they can’t decide what to do next or queues are long), you might not manage everything. There aren’t loads of rides, but the ones they have are fun and kept everyone entertained. Overall, really good experience and worth it for families!

title: Babylon Park London
review_text:
We didn’t book ahead but paid on the day.
We had a push chair, but there is a lift on certified access

A large variety of rides, games and other things to do on site.
We booked for two hours and I think you can easily do the hour and half slot.
Quite a few of the rides and machines were broken which was frustrating.
The site is on two levels but the bottom floor is the one with the most items and rides.
I would highly recommend this

title: Babylon Park London
review_text:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Babylon Park was absolutely brilliant from start to finish! Such a unique concept having an underground theme park right in London — it feels like you’ve stepped into another world the moment you walk in. The neon lights and futuristic vibe make it exciting before you’ve even gone on a ride.

There’s a great mix of attractions, whether you’re into arcade games, fun rides, or just soaking up the atmosphere. Everything felt well organised, clean, and easy to navigate. The staff were friendly and helpful, which made the whole experience even better.

It’s perfect for families, couples, or even a fun day out with mates. We were genuinely surprised by how much there was to do, and the time flew by. Definitely something different from the usual London activities.

Would 100% recommend and will be coming back again! 🎢✨

title: Babylon Park London
review_text: Nice place for children and worth spending money. Staff is good. Only suggestion, make the wrist band more comfortable while accessing the scanner on games/rides. It's difficult for children to scan everytime. Instead use of card or an ID-card which can be hanged over neck is preferable.

title: Babylon Park London
review_text: We had a great time at Babylon Park as 2 adults attending on a Friday morning. We had the unlimited pass and enjoyed the arcade games, the roller coaster was also much better than expected. The man on the front desk was very helpful and helped us start the experience off right. I saw many reviews about broken games at the event, but I am glad to say this was not our experience!

title: Babylon Park London
review_text: This place is akin to Toy Storys Pizza planet and Honestly I really liked the concept; unlimited rides, claw machines and other arcadey attractions. That said I have two issues with this place, the first being a few of the main rides were out of action, or in the case of working rides some seats were broken so it lead to large queues. Secondly one staff member was managing/operating multiple rides at once leading to other rides being shut or long waits whilst they jumped between managing each section individually, this meant eating into your paid, play time, which I suspect is a cost cutting decision at the expense of our money. You should also note that it is quite expensive for what you get when you consider the price of a ticket (£30 per child for 1.5 hours) to an actual outdoor theme park, but overall a decent experience which my children enjoyed.

title: Babylon Park London
review_text: We had a fantastic time with our family, our 4-year-old was happy the entire time, which says it all! The staff were consistently kind and helpful, and a special shout-out to Paulina, who is an absolute rockstar. We’d definitely recommend this place for a fun and enjoyable couple of hours during a trip to London.

title: Emslie Horniman's Pleasance Park

title: Guy Street Park
review_text: Nice little oasis near the guy’s hospital. Very handy to walk through to get to the hospital. Good for summer to escape the heat and have your takeaway lunch there. title: Guy Street Park
review_text: Nice to sit and relax with a sandwich at lunchtime or take the children to play. Interesting to see it mature and flower during the seasons.

review_text: Park is massive so best way to see the most of it is via bike. Granted the bike is limited to certain cycle trails which mean you don't get to go into the actual gardens and fountains.

title: Guy Street Park
review_text: The eerie echo of a basketball intermittently resonating off of the adjacent council houses and car park lends a stark, opaque, and urbanized sensation that a Tron-esque battle to the death is taking place. With that said, it does seem to be a middle ground for all classes, with anyone from Tristan to Tyler, Tabitha to Tanya being made to feel welcome. Suitable for children and/or low key drug deals. Pigeons included, but not necessarily inclusive.

title: Guy Street Park

title: Q-Park Leicester Square
review_text: DO NOT PARK HERE! Last Tuesday my car was another victim. Not only was my car broken into but also the car next to it and another on the next level up. 52 pounds for the pleasure. Really not impressed. I wish I had seen these reviews before parking here. They got my handbag and all its contents. There was no attendant and no one to help when I tried to use the intercom.

title: Q-Park Leicester Square
review_text:
I had an extremely upsetting experience during my recent trip to London, and I feel compelled to warn others. I rented a car from [Company Name] and parked in one of their lots—only to return and find the vehicle had been broken into. Over $20,000 worth of gold jewelry was stolen from me. Other personal items were stolen as well. 2 credit cards charged at a store

What makes this situation even worse is how little the company has done in response. After researching, I discovered that car break-ins are a recurring problem with this company, and yet they’ve done absolutely nothing to improve security or protect their customers. No cameras, no personnel, and zero urgency when reporting incidents like this.

Their response was dismissive and unhelpful—no apology, no effort to investigate, and certainly no accountability.

If you’re planning a trip to London or renting a vehicle, I strongly advise looking elsewhere. Your safety and peace of mind are worth far more than what this company provides.

title: Q-Park Leicester Square
review_text:
DISGUSTING – AVOID AT ALL COSTS.
No attendant, no support, and absolutely no sense of security. We ended up speaking to the Metropolitan Police, who explicitly told us they strongly recommend nobody use this car park as it is a known target for crime. They also said Q-Park is aware and does not care. To make matters worse, the entire place reeks of stale urine. I only wish I had read the reviews before parking here. Never again.

title: Q-Park Leicester Square
review_text: Unfortunately, our car was broken into in a parking lot. My daughter was so scared that she was trembling and couldn’t even cry. All our luggage was stolen. Later, we were fortunate to receive help from Lewis, Daniel, and Ade. ❤️Lewis informed me that they had found our luggage, and Daniel assisted me throughout the process of reporting to the police and contacting the rental car company. ❤️🙏During my most helpless moments, these two heroes were there to support me. The next day, when we returned to the parking lot, Ade helped me confirm the luggage. Encountering such kind-hearted people in the UK deeply moved me… Please remember not to leave items in your car, and I hope this wonderful country can soon regain its peace and safety.🙏🙏🙏

title: Q-Park Leicester Square

They will of course take no responsibility for the incident

title: Q-Park Leicester Square

This place is disgraceful. You think it is a private car park but anyone can walk in - there are loads of delivery drivers and shady characters due to some sort of bicycle parking space inside the car park. No security and the place stinks of urine. Also a super tight underground car park - can barely fit a mini in there. You are better off parking on the street.

title: Q-Park Leicester Square
review_text: Used this parking when we went to Covent Gardens for the Nutcracker ballet. Location is convenient just a short walk from the Theatre. Parking spaces are very small. It’s tight to move around in there. I had prepaid, but the system didn’t recognize my receipt so I had to pay on exit again. I contacted them afterwards and I did get my refund, but it was Inconvenient and alarming.

title: Q-Park Leicester Square
review_text: APPALING! It is embarrassing that such a company can requiere premium prices for services - £31 - for less than 3 hours - advertising themselves as award winning with super quality services -only to deny any responsibility in regards to their duty of care when the advertised security measures failed. My car was broken into - backpack stolen( mind you it was not in sight) and my window has still not been fixed since the 12th of February. They refused any form of efficient communication - providing me only with standard- automated-like responses - and when I called the number recommended - I was told this is customer support - and since this is a complain - the only way I can communicate would be through emails. They refused to let me know why my parking card was retained at exit - and also to share the CCTV footage with me. They have only advised me to claim any damage with insurance - so that I can deal with the premiums myself later. The way I was treated - appalling! The main answer - was this is a public space - we assume no responsibility - regardless of the cameras or barriers they advertise! Needless to say, I was not the only one with a broken car that night! IT IS ABSOLUTELY HORRIFIC THAT SUCH SERVICE PROVIDES ARE ALLOWED TO ASK PREMIUM SERVICES FOR LOCATIONS THEY ADVERTISE AS PRIVATE AND SECURE - ONLY TO DENY ANY RESPOSABILITY AND REVERT TO "IT'S A PUBLIC SPACE! ACCEPT IT! YOU ARE NOT THE ONLY ONE!"

title: NCP London Covent Garden
review_text:
This is now Apex Parking and they seem to have invested a bit having taken over from NCP.
- Security is good with a friendly & helpful attendant from 6am to 10pm. The shutters are both fully functional and the pedestrian access has a code entry to prevent casual intruders.
- Good value for central London.
- Take care selecting your space. There are lots with projecting pipes and ductwork.
- Yes it still smells, that’s going to take some time to fade!

title: NCP London Covent Garden
review_text:
Big space lift to street level,lovely and clean.
Went to theatre yesterday great wheelchair access in car park .
Re-entry via key pad for your registration number and security man in car park.
One thing I would say being London with traffic road closures and road works allow extra time to get there.
Not a fault of the car park and most definitely would recommend and use again.
Ohh there is a high restriction so if your higher than a transit van with a roof rack don't try it like the bloke did yesterday!!

title: NCP London Covent Garden
review_text:
Obviously not looking at the reviews (who does for car parks) but this seriously needs to be sorted.
Came back to my car last night to have the back windows smashed in!! The same as everyone else on this review list. £2,500 worth of goods were stolen along with personal belongings.
We had left the car at 19:15 and came back to the car at 21:10 so this was ONLY a 2hr window. We informed the police and we have spoke to an individual that was on the entrance of the car park when returning.
We were originally told there was no CCTV but as you can see in the images below we have found CCTV via the entrance with the automatic doors, the side streets along the car park and at the barriers. which would clearly show what people came in and did this and should be punished for doing this.
I will be contacting ncp tomorrow morning to request cctv footage of this time frame!
Absolutely appalling!
Will not be staying in a car park like this again!

title: NCP London Covent Garden
review_text:
Extortionately priced.

Collecting my car after 10pm was a real headache as the signage was wrong and told me to collect my car via the main ramp entrance which was locked. The signage says to discuss with a member of staff if you need to collect your car out of hours however no one was available and there’s no number to call. Absolute nightmare. I managed to get my car having gone back up to the main pedestrian entrance, which was in fact open despite signage claiming otherwise.

Definitely one to avoid in the future.

title: NCP London Covent Garden
review_text:
Where do I start. The smell of urine literally slaps you in the throat it's ridiculous. There is no signage whatsoever. So if you need to leave it's the top floor.

Do better and clean it...people are paying for this.

title: NCP London Covent Garden
review_text:
I wish I read the comments before I decided to park my car here, my car was broken into, no CCTV footage, when I went to report to the police, they informed me it was a daily occurrence and NCP never do anything to help the situation.
Luckily it was a worthless work bag however my ID badge was stolen. Was lucky I didn’t have any valuables in the car, thank god my disabled badge, Wallet was safe within the other compartments of my car.
If you value your windows/ cars, just park on the street!
Anything is better than this happening.
Utterly appalled at NCP - thought I was the only one initially until the police had informed me. Luckily no one was hurt, but now I have to deal with potential ID theft!
Please DO NOT park here!!!!!!

I’ve just filed a complaint with NCP - to install CCTV, hope this happens!!!!

title: NCP London Covent Garden
review_text: Do not use.  We have been burgled. Car smashed and everything covered in urine. Valuables taken   Dozens of cars done.  No cameras and no one cared.   Ruined my birthday

title: Euro Car Parks
review_text:
I paid £60 to this company after staying 18 minutes over a 1 hour ticket. I had already paid £1.90 for parking.

I remained in the car because my young child was asleep. I waited for my child to wake up. That was the only reason for the delay.

There was no obstruction. No misuse of the space. No attempt to avoid payment.

This £60 represents a full week of food for a single mother and her child.

If you park here, be aware:
• ANPR cameras track your entry and exit to the second
• Even a short delay can result in a charge
• Personal circumstances will not be taken into account

Think carefully before using this car park.

title: Euro Car Parks
review_text:
SUCH A GOOD CAR PARK!!!!

So many fun fun massive cars with like 50 seats in them, crazy how big cars can be in the UK

In Belarus they only fit 5 people MAX!

title: Euro Car Parks
review_text: The car park is easy to find and has quiet some spaces considering it's location. But beside this is expensive considering it doesn't even offer a toilet or security in case someone breaks into your car. There are two machines for ticket one accepts only euros the other british pounds. Only card payment. It's very expensive for coaches offering only 12 or 24 hour charge nothing between. Closest tube station is Queensway and Notting Hill Gate. Bus stop next to car park.

title: Euro Car Parks
review_text:
Euro Car Parks are an absolute disgrace. I'm a disabled driver with a wheelchair user, and they fined me despite paying for parking.
Why? I arrived 8 minutes early and left 8 minutes late. That's the time it takes to get a wheelchair in and out of the car. It's not optional—it's necessary.
I paid for my parking. I appealed and explained my disability. They rejected it anyway.
Euro Car Parks don't care about disabled people. They don't care about basic decency. They only care about money.
They should be ashamed, but clearly they have no shame. Heartless, greedy, and completely unfit to operate parking facilities used by vulnerable people.
Avoid them at all costs. They are an embarrassment.

title: Euro Car Parks
review_text:
The ticket machine in a car park was not working. I was issued with a ticket and appealed, but I did not receive a reply to my appeal, despite signalling that I had not. They still issued the maximum fine and gave little opportunity to interact with anyone.

Their customer service is nonexistent and there team does not respond to emails in time and does not take responsibility for this.

Avoid their car parks

title: Euro Car Parks
review_text:
Broken machine – feels like a setup to issue fines.
Parked for 35 minutes at Sacred Heart Church Cobham on 31st October. It was pouring with rain and the payment machine didn’t work despite several attempts. I planned to pay when leaving but forgot — only because the machine failed in the first place. Now I’m fined £100 for their broken system. Feels intentional, as if the broken machine is just a way to trap people into paying fines. Absolutely disgraceful. Disgraceful and unfair.

title: Euro Car Parks
review_text: The payment machine at Sacred Heart Church, Cobham, was not working when I parked. I was unable to make a payment and was later issued a fine. This was an honest mistake caused entirely by the faulty machine. The company should have been aware of the issue and not penalised customers for a problem beyond their control.

title: NCP London Marylebone Road Car Park
place_title: NCP London Marylebone Road Car Park
review_text:
IF YOU WANT TO GET RID OF YOUR MOTORBIKE- TAKE THIS CAR PARK- ORGANISED CRIME

I am from Germany and my wife and I were on holiday the 1st two weeks of July- we were travelling all along the country by motorbike and nothing happened.  As we heard before that BMW motorbikes are at the moment very valued by thieves we booked our stays according to safe and protected parking spaces. Our last two nights were booked in a hotel in London so we could reach our time slot for the Eurotunnel in a relaxed journey. As our hotel in London was near the Marylebone Car Park and was recommended from the doorman at the hotel we had really no cause for distrust in this car park. And there were cameras and a gate and a porter! Well surprise, surprise! On Saturay morning, 12th of July at 6 am I wanted to take the motorbike to get packed BUT nothing was there. Our motorbike was stolen, I still can`t believe it. We arrived on Thursday in the afternoon and on Saturay morning 6 am it was already stolen! Nobody, really nobody can tell me this is an coincidence in this rather short timeframe !!!! We went back to the hotel to call the police but they were unavailable till 9 am ( thankfully I got not stabbed ) and as my wife gathered not very interested. It seems the cameras are not working and the NICE staff who let me into the garage on Thursday was also unavailable.
Needless to say we lost except our motorbike ( 2 month old ) our timeslot for the Eurotunnel, had to book any flight we could get to Germany and had to send our bike clothes as the side suitcases from the bike via DHL to Germany. It was really a lot of trouble we had on top of everything. And it went on back home with all the correspondence to the German police and the insurances.  If just some wishes my wife made will come true, those thives and all included will never have a good day in their miserable lifes.

title: NCP London Marylebone Road Car Park
place_title: NCP London Marylebone Road Car Park
review_text:
I frequently see homeless people and smashed glass all around the car park. As a female, I do not feel safe walking into this car park on my own after dark, let alone park on the lower floor. Plus, NCP decided to sell the front row spots to SixT last week. They did not bother notifying their existing season ticket holders. Instead a security guard told me that the spot I was parked on now belonged to SixT and NCP was about to issue me a fine! Great customer retention strategy!

I am too scared to park downstairs, there is no CCTV, and the security guard is useless because he is always watching something on his phone in the office and could not care less.

When the entrance gate is broken, I have to wait 10 min to get his attention by banging my phone on the metal gate (there is no emergency number to contact him on obviously, and their helpline is 9-5 Mon to Fri). The gates are always always broken, and NCP started leaving signs to say that the back gate is open - this makes me feel even less secure, both in terms of walking into the car part and parking there.

title: ParkBee Green Parking - St Augustine Car Park
place_title: ParkBee Green Parking - St Augustine Car Park
review_text:
Very convenient car park close to Hammersmith station, making it an excellent option if you’re travelling into central London. Easy to find, well located, and ideal for hopping straight onto the Tube without hassle.

The parking process was smooth and straightforward, and the location saves a lot of stress compared to driving further into the city. Definitely a great choice for anyone looking for a practical and well-placed car park near Hammersmith.

title: ParkBee Green Parking - St Augustine Car Park
place_title: ParkBee Green Parking - St Augustine Car Park
review_text:
My experience with this parking place is very good.
I was searcing for a car park near to eventimapollo theatre. I found this car park after some online search. I was asked to install ParkBee app to be able to reserve in advance. I installed the app and bought a slot of 5 hours on the date of event.
The session is visible on the app. It shows the remaining time of your parking. You may arrive the car park before your booked time and may start session on tha app. If you arrive late, the session starts automatically at the time of booking, which is fine.
When you arrive there, you'll see a gate. From the ParkBee app, you may open it and enter the car park.
It is very small and available for 15-20 cars. I believe when you buy in advance, a parking place is reserved for you. You may buy after you arrive but it is risky. You may not find available space.
When you leave the carpark, again from the app you may end your session and open the gate.
It is not expensive. I paid £15 for 5 hours of stay.
This carpark is highly recommended if you need to park your vehicle in the area. I would use it again in a similar ocassion.

title: ParkBee Green Parking - St Augustine Car Park
place_title: ParkBee Green Parking - St Augustine Car Park
review_text:

I phoned customer service twice, and spoke to who I believe were two different women, that did an incredible job being reassuring and determined in resolving our parking issues and turned what was a stressful situation (thinking I'd have to pay a lot to park at the hotel across the road) into worry-free experience.

Car park is very small so make sure to plan in advance to secure a spot. The area seemed very secure.

title: ParkBee Green Parking - St Augustine Car Park

title: YourParkingSpace
review_text: Should have warning prior to entering. - warning wide cars. Drove a tesla in, the sides are so high on the way out. Did thousands of pounds worth of damage. £35 approx to park for 5 hours and a massive repair job.

title: YourParkingSpace
review_text: Good location and secure 24/7 manned security + CCTV. Tight getting in and out but it was easy enough. Some bigger SUVs cars were parked there. Pricey but what do you expect for central London. Would use again.

title: YourParkingSpace
review_text:
I recently parked at this car park and had a mixed experience. The location is undoubtedly convenient, but the parking space itself was quite challenging. The entry and exit were extremely tight, making it difficult to maneuver without risking a scuff on my car's rims. It was a nerve-wracking experience, especially in such a busy area.

Additionally, the pricing was on the higher side, which I understand given the central London location. However, I felt that the cost didn't quite justify the hassle of navigating the cramped space. If you're looking for a quick and easy parking option, I would recommend considering other alternatives unless you're willing to deal with the tight conditions or if you have a compact car. Overall, while the location is a plus, the difficulties in accessing the parking space made for a less-than-ideal experience.

title: YourParkingSpace
review_text:
Amazing value for money considering how central it is. A lot cheaper than surrounding car parks, and since it's under the Holiday Inn there are plenty of people around so it feels more secure. Also there's security.

Only issue is that it is tight getting in and out, so large SUVs or estate cars would struggle.

title: YourParkingSpace
review_text:
At the time this was the cheapest “secured parking” in the area via the app. As I was also staying in the Holiday Inn, it was more than convenient and entry to the parking is via the hotel drop down zone.
On the day of arrival, I checked my email for any prerequisites before turning up. I seemed to have missed the part about car size restrictions. They stated “a medium sized car and a maximum height of 183cm” which unfortunately my BMW X7 may exceed the limit of. To my surprise, my car just about fit going down the ramp and I lowered the suspension as well just in case of height issues. Manoeuvring around was also very tight but doable. The space that was available was also large enough and there where more others but not many. This is just a pre warning for others with large SUV’s. Come here at your own risk and be aware of your surroundings. Other than that, my car was kept safe and I drove away happy with my car unscathed waving goodbye to the attendant on the way out.
Also, there are restaurants and a supermarket within walking distance.

title: YourParkingSpace
review_text: Although the bays were of normal to big size to accommodate a big/wide car, the exit is so narrow especially with a curve that I was struggling to get out. The attendant had to come out of his cabin to guide me out of the place. AVOID THIS CAR PARK IF YOU HAVE A LONG CAR.

title: YourParkingSpace
review_text: Great value for location (under the Holiday Inn Hotel with a lift into the lobby) and highly valuable comfort knowing there is security in place. Unfortunately, makes it worthwhile driving to London as opposed to taking the train from anywhere more than 1 hour away :)

review_text:
Super nice place to ride if it's rainy outside. Quite easy to find, open from 12 to 10 everyday, guy at the entrance is really kind.
Be careful if you ride bmx or scooter, helmet are required, if you don't have one you can rent one for 3£

title: BAYSIXTY6 Skate Park
