review_text: Fantastic skatepark - the best one we’ve been too an d we’ve been to dozens! It’s great in any weather as it’s under the bridge, lots of tunes and rides for all levels. Great classes for kids too during dedicated time when there are few adults. Highly recommended! Will keep coming back.

title: BAYSIXTY6 Skate Park
review_text:
Bay 66

Missing stars because the staff don’t give you the time of day, they are quite rude. Maybe I just had a negative experience, it was probably just a one off.
I skateboarded here once and never been here again.
It is full of posh teenagers or toddlers most of the time.

Brooklyn Beckham has been seen here. Definitely a spot for teenagers to try really hard to seem cool and edgy. I would avoid.
Scruffy jeans, gold chains, and AirPods are the current style here.

I would say this place is only good for toddlers/young children because there are exclusive sessions, so they will not be knocked off. Also lots of other parents standing around watching their children or helping them maintain balance. It’s a good place to start out because there are some easy ramps.

There is a toilet, a café and a reception. You can buy skateboard paraphernalia here too; grips, stickers, wheels etc.

If you are a keen skateboarder thinking of checking this place out, you’ll probably prefer Meanwhile gardens or South Bank. But this place is most suitable for beginners.

title: BAYSIXTY6 Skate Park
review_text: Fantastic skate park, Good staff, Only negative could be made fully indoor as whether disturbances Do you cause a problem. But nothing we own can do

title: Nora Cafe
review_text: It was my last day in London before going back to Malaysia and I never really experienced a full english before. Nora Cafe popped up on while I was searching for restaurants and the pictures looked promising. It's safe to say I wasn't disappointed. The Cafe was cosy and warm. The server was friendly. Although the food came out very quick, it was nice and hot. Every bit of the dish was great. The sausage and bacon were decent but the black pudding was surprisingly good. The hashbrowns were crispy on the outside and fluffy on the inside. The eggs had runny yolks just how I like them. The fried bread is a must. The beans were alright. I was given buttered toast and a cup of tea too. The toast was light but crisp. It paired well with the beans. To top it off, the price was fairly reasonable. I would come here again and recommend this place to my friends and family if they ever visit London.

title: Barbican Cafe
review_text: We had breakfast here on the first day of our London trip and absolutely loved it! Naturally, we had to come back a few days later. I highly recommend the Eggs Benedict, Çılbır, and the Turkish breakfast—they were all delicious. The staff were incredibly kind. Thank you for everything! 🌸💕

title: Barbican Cafe
review_text: The food looks out of the world, but the best part is tastes even better. The staff is very warm and courteous. Loved the vibe! Def going to visit again and recommend it to my friends! Keep up the good work! ✌️

title: Kennington Lane Cafe
review_text:
I had a wonderful experience at this place. The staff were extremely friendly, kind, and always smiling, which made the whole visit even more enjoyable. The service was quick and professional, and they really made us feel welcome.

The food was absolutely delicious. Everything was fresh, well-prepared, and full of flavor. On top of that, the portions were very generous, so you definitely get great value for your money.

I highly recommend this place to anyone looking for tasty food, a warm atmosphere, and excellent service. I will definitely come back again!

title: Kennington Lane Cafe
review_text:
Absolutely loved this place! The food was incredible — a proper, hearty breakfast cooked to perfection. Everything was fresh, full of flavour, and beautifully presented. You can tell they really care about quality.

But what truly made the experience stand out was the service. The team was warm, attentive, and genuinely welcoming from the moment we walked in. They made us feel comfortable, looked after, and added such a positive vibe to the whole visit.

Great food, amazing service, and a lovely atmosphere — I will definitely be coming back!

title: Fair Shot Cafe
review_text:
It’s increíble initiatives! I have visited with friends, only to support this project and I’m absolutely delighted and highly recommended this place!

The people are super nice, very friendly, calm and supportive. The coffee and matcha are very good.
The sandwiches are delicious and huge.

It’s great enough to stop over to have a drink or even breakfast.

The place where you may donate and support the project. Or just buy a cup apart.

title: Fair Shot Cafe
review_text: I loved the cafe’s concept. It feels great to support a place that trains adults with disabilities to get a job. Concept aside, the interior of the cafe was cozy and inviting. The food and coffee selection was reasonably varied. Staff was helpful and smiley which added to the experience. Overall, this place rocks and is well worth a visit!

title: SO Café London
address: Forecourt, Maida Vale Station, Randolph Ave., London W9 1JS
review_text:
It says best speciality coffee in Maida Vale,

It is actually true, they use Organic good quality coffee beans.

The Cream Doughnut is very light and fresh, not very sweet and I am addicted to their Chocolate Brownies, very fudgy and rich in Chocolate.

Congratulations for your new seating area outside the Station, it looks very nice.

Christmas Cups already!

title: SO Café London
address: Forecourt, Maida Vale Station, Randolph Ave., London W9 1JS
review_text: Thank you so much for your help! I'm very happy to report that my son is back and well!

title: SO Café London
address: Forecourt, Maida Vale Station, Randolph Ave., London W9 1JS
review_text: Best coffee shop in London. The worker is very friendly and polite. A must visit for any coffee or donuts lovers. The Brownies are out of this world

title: SO Café London
address: Forecourt, Maida Vale Station, Randolph Ave., London W9 1JS
review_text: The sign said best coffee shop in Maida Vale so I had to try. Doughnut was delicious and absolutely packed with Nutella. The coffee was delicious and well made. Had a good laugh with Pierrot who was manning the shop. No complaints, well deserved 5 stars! Looking forward to coming back

title: SO Café London
address: Forecourt, Maida Vale Station, Randolph Ave., London W9 1JS
review_text: Super cute, by a nice underground mosaic just by the Abbey Road crosswalk if you are coming by tube. Great coffee, good donuts. Excellent service, very kind guy.

title: Small square cafe
review_text: Absolutely love this cafe and am so glad it’s here! Delicious grab and go chia pudding, fresh juices, breakfast sandwiches, baked goods, and the best darned gluten free cannoli I’ve ever had! I came here daily for a week straight and never got tired of it.

title: Small square cafe
review_text: Very cute cafe at the corner. We went when it’s opened and it was already a bit crowd to take a coffee inside. Had a coffe and banna bread and it was great!

title: Small square cafe
review_text: Super quaint place filled with excellent pastries and hot breakfast options. Very friendly staff. Excellent coffee.  I had the ham and cheese baguette and it was fresh and crisp.

title: Small square cafe
review_text:
So glad we found Little Square Cafe. They have fuelled us with delicious coffee and porridge all week on our London adventures.
Service is speedy & friendly. Lots of vegan and gluten free choices. This place is a true 💎if I could give more stars I would 😀

title: Small square cafe
review_text:
MOST AMAZING COFFEE IN THE EAST!
A beautiful gem 💎
I was served EACH time I came by phenomenal ladies named Milena and Sylvia and I am always happy to come back. All the food is fresh and delicious, but the coffee !!! God, an absolute must try !! I don’t understand how isn’t this place heaving all the time !!

title: Small square cafe
review_text: Small Square Caffe, a beautiful place where you can quiet your thoughts with its lovely green décor. Food made with love and given to the customer with love. Wonderful coffee that will energize your morning. Lunch prepared with passion that will leave your stomach perfectly satisfied. And above all, amazing service — people whose smiles are contagious and make your day better. I wholeheartedly recommend this place.♥️♥️♥️

title: Small square cafe
review_text: Just came in randomly and unexpectedly, but this place was so cozy and nice. Warm welcome and have a good quality of the croissant and coffee. Definitely come back and I’ll try the pistachio croissant and ice latte next time.

title: Caffe In
review_text:
I recently visited Cafe In and honestly what a cute wholesome little spot. They have an amazing variety of dishes so there is plenty to choose from, and everything we tried was so flavoursome and fresh. They also have a lovely selection of cakes which makes it very hard to leave without something sweet.

The staff were genuinely warm and welcoming which made the whole visit even nicer. Such a cosy little place and I would definitely recommend it if you want somewhere relaxed with great food.

title: Caffe In
review_text: Located in shepherds market just off Piccadilly. Stopped by for a quick bite to eat. Came at the end of the day so obviously not very busy. Nice choice of food at reasonable prices. We had the salmon fish cake and the curried chicken. Food tasted very nice. Service was friendly and helpful.

title: Caffe In
review_text: a lovely lady greeted me with a smile the moment I entered, she was very friendly, and offered good options for the breakfast. it was great, delicious and filling, coffee was good too. I really enjoyed my meals at this place.

title: Caffe In
review_text: Excellent!  Has vegan options!  The food is so good. My wife had the traditional egg breakfast and loved it!

title: Caffe In
review_text: I ordered a brioche bun with truffled egg, bacon and onions. The brioche was tasty and the grilled onions really added extra flavour. However I could barely taste any truffle, which was quite disappointing considering it cost 14 quid. The service was also a bit slow as they seemed understaffed. It took at least twenty minutes for our food to be served.

title: Caffe In
review_text:
Discovered this Cafe in the heart of Shepherds Market, by chance. Good place for breakfast or a light lunch. One complaint. When we asked for Halloumi as an extra in the salad, for an additional £ 3.50, two small slices of halloumi appeared.

Not particularly cheap, but it is in Mayfair!

title: Caffe In
review_text: We found this lovely little Caffe for breakfast. I had eggs Benedict and my husband had avacado toast. Both were fantastic. Fresh ingredients. The green juice was also quite yummy.

title: Caffe In
review_text:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
A true gem in Shepherd Market!
Cafe Inn is one of my favourite spots — the food is always incredibly fresh, and the menu has a fantastic range of nutritious and healthy options. You can really tell they care about quality.

The staff make every visit even better. Goshia and Eva deserve a special mention for their warmth, professionalism, and consistently superb customer service. They always go the extra mile and create such a welcoming atmosphere.

Highly recommended for anyone looking for great food and exceptional service!
user_name: The Pharmacy at Mayfair and Clinic

title: Cali Cafe - Westminster
address: Constance Court, 39 Church St, London, NW8 8ES
review_text: Such a charming café The ambiance is calm and elegant with comfortable seating and spotless surroundings. The menu offers great variety, and everything looks freshly made. I tried their frappé and a paneer sandwich both tasted fantastic. The frappé was creamy and refreshing while the sandwich was packed with flavor. The staff were friendly and respectful providing excellent service without being intrusive. This café strikes the perfect balance between comfort and quality. I will definitely be recommending it to my friends and visiting often.

title: Cali Cafe - Westminster
address: Constance Court, 39 Church St, London, NW8 8ES
review_text: We visited Cali Cafe for breakfast, being from up North we were pleasantly surprised at the prices - incredibly reasonable for London! I paid £3.40 for scrambled egg on toast, and my coffee was less than £3 and delicious! The menu was plentiful, the service rapid and the staff friendly. Highly recommend.

title: Art Cafe London
address: 17 London Rd, Elephant and Castle, London, SE1 6JX
review_text:
I visited 'Art Café' on a Saturday morning with a couple of friends. We initially sat in the slightly gloomy basement before they moved us up to the ground floor which was nicely decorated and had a pleasant ambience. My friends both had a full English breakfast, while I had the
'Turkish eggs' and a 'club sandwich.
The Turkish eggs were nice and reasonably priced, although perhaps a little bland and under-seasoned. It was served with what seemed like freshly made flatbread which was tasty. My friends had good things to say about the full English, and while it certainly was not cheap at £15, the price simply reflects the rampant inflation of the past few years.

Unfortunately, things took a major turn for the worse with the club sandwich (which was essentially a two-layered bacon, egg and salad sandwich). The bread was soggy and the bacon was very underdone; 1 think it is likely that this was prepared in advance (perhaps even a few days prior) and them microwaved. Worst of all, as seen in the photo, the salad had clearly gone off as most of it was mouldy and turning brown. Judging by the other reviews this doesn't seem to happen often so I can only assume this was a one-off lapse in quality control. Regardless, it did ruin what was otherwise an enjoyable meal.

As a result, I think three stars is a fair representation of the overall experience. It pains me to give an independent café a poor score, but unfortunately this reflects the disappointment that came with the soggy sandwich. I probably wouldn't return as the mouldy salad was very off-putting.

title: Parco Cafe
review_text: Hidden gem alert! I love this charming, family-run cafe in Vauxhall Park, just a stone's throw from Vauxhall and Nine Elms tube stations. With a passion for good quality coffee and fresh food, they serve up delicious treats in a cozy setting. Expect a warm welcoming and good customer service. The menu strikes the perfect balance between simplicity and variety, offering an array of cakes, baked goods, and satisfying meals like full English breakfasts, paninis, omelettes, and salads (including veggie options). Standout dishes like 'Eggs à la Parco' and their unique iced-matcha with a hint of cinnamon make this cafe a must-visit. Highly recommended!

title: Becks Cafe
review_text: Becks is such a lovely little cafe. The food was genuinely delicious, fresh, comforting, and clearly made with care. But what really made the visit special was the staff. They work so hard and still manage to be warm, friendly, and welcoming, which makes you feel instantly at home. You can really feel the love they put into the place. I’ll definitely be back. ( also handy because it has a downstairs area for when upstairs is full.)

title: Becks Cafe
review_text: Amazing little cafe, excellent food and attention to detail. My wife and I came here for a late breakfast and the staff noticed that we were celebrating our 12 year wedding anniversary. They came over to congratulate us. Impeccable service. We'll be making another visit as soon as we can. Thank you

title: Cafe Terrace
review_text:
The Cafe is small but led by big hearted ladies! The greatings were warm and you made feel very welcome. Coffee was delicious. The gluten free options on the menu were great opportunity for me to try Colombian delicacies all homemade. The prices are very resonable despite is just round a corner from King's Cross Station. Do yourself a favour and if like myself you love food and different cuisines visit the place. There is also vast choice of sandwiches and tasty smoothies.
I will see you next time I'm in London!

title: Cafe Terrace
review_text: I adore this place, the owner is so sweet and genuine as is everyone else working there. I often visit for empanadas and coffee but I have also had great experiences with their other food. It’s a little place but very cozy.

title: Haggerston Perk Cafe
review_text:
Haggerston Perk Cafe — Coffee with a Cause

Located near Hackney City Farm, this café is more than just a place to refuel — it’s a social enterprise supporting people with disabilities through the Tower Project, making every visit meaningful.

Why it’s a great option:

Excellent coffee — smooth espresso, oat milk options, and seasonal specials

Tasty bites — fresh pastries, sandwiches, and light lunch options

Warm atmosphere — peaceful, friendly, and ideal for solo visits or casual meetups

Community-driven — inclusive staff and a mission that matters

title: Mama's Cafe.
review_text: Got the breakfast stack here and let me tell you, it was absolutely delicious. A hidden gem on the streets of London if this was my last meal on Earth I'd leave the world happy. Do yourself a favor and get your breakfast here if you are ever in the area. Or travel to go here, it's worth it.

title: Cake Hole Cafe
review_text: The best scones we've had! We loved visiting this little Cake Hole Cafe. It has a gorgeous vintage and eclectic vibe and the staff who run the cafe are just wonderful - so friendly and warm. The cakes and sweets are incredible and all homemade! We had the best cream tea here which was so special because we had friends visiting internationally. It's attached to a vintage store which you can enjoy after a delicious bite. A lot of love and care has gone into this humble cafe and we cannot wait to go back.

title: Cake Hole Cafe
review_text: Read good reviews about the scones here and visited to try them out. They certainly didn’t disappoint! Both the fruit and plain scones were delicious and the raspberry jam was particularly lovely. The other cakes and pastries looked delicious as well. The interior was cosy and quaint with a lovely atmosphere. Highly recommend!

title: Regency Cafe
review_text:
Amazing English breakfast! The welcome is absolutely top, super friendly and warm from the moment you arrive. Everything is delicious, well cooked, and really satisfying. And most importantly: don’t change the prices they’re perfect and honestly make you want to order even more.

As tourists, this was truly our best London experience. We went there almost every morning during our stay and never got tired of it. 100% recommended!

title: Regency Cafe
review_text: Absolutely class, the breakfast was really good but the place itself, the people, the light, were the best part of all. Wherever you are in London it is worth the trip, I'd recommend going early, specially the obvious days of the week.

title: Regency Cafe
review_text: Great way to end my London trip! A no frills English Diner. Hearty portions and friendly staff! They take time of they know you're a tourist if you have any  questions.

title: THE ROCHESTER CAFE
review_text: Great find on my short trip to London. Had breakfast with a friend; coffee and food were great. Well spiced, balanced flavours. Great selection of pastries and lunch dishes were already out! Service is quick and the staff are attentive.

title: THE ROCHESTER CAFE
review_text:
Without a doubt the best breakfast we had on our entire trip. We got the Eggs Benedict, and the Egg Royal. I can highly recommend both.

I also got a almond croissant and that was amazing.

title: THE ROCHESTER CAFE
review_text: A charming and friendly place, perfect for a cozy meal despite being a bit small. The staff were attentive, and the food was tasty – definitely worth a visit.

title: The Warehouse Cafe SE16
review_text: Went for the first time and ordered breakfast, liked that you can choose your own items at a reasonable price, with tea/coffee and bread included. Helpful lady taking the order at the till, food did take quite a while to arrive as it seems everything is freshly cooked though some items were lukewarm, but no catering sausage which is also a bonus at a cafe. Not much seating if the place is busy though there is an upstairs. Would I go back? Probably but I wouldn't go out of my way.

title: Cafe de Provence
review_text: First time we came was in December of 2024– the food was delicious and it wasn’t as busy so the staff was very attentive making the atmosphere more comfortable and friendly. Came here again October 2025 and it was a whole different experience. The server lady was standoffish and a bit rude, especially when it came to ordering and seating. The full English breakfast was very greasy and we asked for poached eggs but were given fried eggs. When we brought it up she just stared at us so we said it’s fine, accidents happen. Couldn’t finish the food. Just too greasy and we felt unwelcome so wanted to leave asap. There was a dispute with the staff and a walk-in customer as well. As soon as that customer walked out, the staff was loudly bad mouthing him. It was just too much going on at once. I usually never visit a restaurant twice, but I loved it here so much the first time that I wanted to support it again. Bad decision unfortunately. There are lots of other halal English breakfast options in London if that’s what you’re looking for.

title: Cafe de Provence
review_text:
🌟🌟 FAVOURITE CAFE IN LONDON! 🌟🌟

The food is very authentic and tasty. The buffet lunch is amazing and feels homemade like my mum’s cooking! Lots of vegetarian and healthy options. Sabrina and Ben are so warm, friendly and always make me feel welcome. I highly recommend this cafe to anyone visiting Covent Garden / London.

Will be back very soon! 😊

title: Cafe de Provence
review_text: This is a hidden gem in London. Coffee, croissants, wraps and breakfasts were all sampled and all excellent. The staff and owner were so friendly that we felt as if we were with family. My favourite find in London! A must visit as superb value for exceptional food.

title: Cafe 101
review_text: Such a nice refuge away from the tourist traps. Ran by the Salvation Army and a great value, with very tasty food.

title: Usagi Anime Maid Cafe
review_text:
Absolutely amazing Maid Cafe experience. A must visit if you're keen on the theatre of maids doting on you.

5/5 for the atmosphere and staff, I felt like a proper Ojou-sama 🥰

title: Usagi Anime Maid Cafe
review_text:
The food is alright, but that isn't why anyone is visiting here. I just felt bad eating away the ketchup art done ontop of my omurice. Cute maids, and a wholesome experience pushing through mountains of embarrassment spouting some Japanese nonsense - but genuinely very nice people! If you were already searching for something along these lines then it's probably exactly what you want.

Izumi was a good server :)

title: Double six cafe
review_text: My first visit before catching a train. The staff are very friendly and attentive. Quick service, extensive menu and 'hearty' meals. Very reasonable opening hours and a lot cheaper than the station!

title: Double six cafe
review_text:
If you’re in the area and looking for a relaxed spot to enjoy a delicious breakfast, The Double Six Cafe, located at 66 Eversholt Street, London NW1 1BL, is definitely worth a visit. Just a 10-minute walk from Euston Station, this modern, minimalist café offers a warm and inviting atmosphere, perfect for a casual meal.

I had the scrambled eggs on toast, which were light, fluffy, and well-seasoned, paired with golden, crispy toast—simple but very satisfying. My friend opted for the No. 2 breakfast, which included eggs, bacon, sausage, plum tomatoes, mushrooms, and fried bread. It was a hearty and classic choice, perfect for those craving a traditional, filling breakfast.

The service was fast, friendly, and attentive without being intrusive. The café has a relaxed vibe, making it a great place to unwind while enjoying quality food.

Overall, The Double Six Cafe is a gem in the area for travellers.

title: Pottery Cafe
review_text: I came here for the first time over 10 years ago, the atmosphere and love put into this cafe hasn’t changed at all. I brought my best friend here for her 21st and it felt like i was a little kid again. The staff were so friendly and so, so kind. They were amazing to speak to and very welcoming. There were two lovely girls, one who made such a delicious blueberry matcha for me and another who gave my best friend a complimentary heart to paint and this very sweet guy with glasses who was very patient with me after I asked a million questions and advice. The atmosphere is fantastic, super cosy, great vibes, whoever was controlling the playlist has great taste, they know ball ;p I definitely want to come here again even if it’s just for a cup of coffee

title: Pottery Cafe
review_text:
Visited Pottery Café in Fulham for one of their adults-only evening sessions and had such a great time.

The space is really calm and aesthetic, with chilled music and a relaxed vibe that makes it easy to switch off and just enjoy painting. We chose from their Emma Bridgewater pottery, which you can only paint here, and it was surprisingly therapeutic.

We had an iced matcha and a chai latte, plus a gingerbread man and red velvet cake. Everything was freshly baked and perfect for a low-key evening.

It's also pet friendly, which is a really nice touch, even though we didn't bring our dog this time.

Such a fun alternative to after-work drinks or a casual date night. Would definitely go back.

title: Pottery Cafe
review_text:
An interesting and inspiring experience 🎨 – painting tableware with a great selection, from cups to flower vases. The atmosphere is lovely, with everyone around being creative, which makes it even more motivating. Even though I’m far from being an artist 🧑‍🎨😅, I truly enjoyed the process of painting. On top of that, they serve delicious tea and coffee.

The staff is very friendly and welcoming.

Definitely a place worth visiting if you want to unwind and dedicate some time to creativity ☺️

title: Clubhouse Cafe 2
review_text: Visited this little cafe an early morning. Had a flat white and a pain au chocolat. Prices are very reasonable. Coffee was lovely and atmosphere was great. Losing one star because the croissant could have been fresher. The chocolate inside was solid and generally as fresh.

title: Clubhouse Cafe 2
review_text: Visiting London Bridge area so, called in for a quick coffee and sandwich. Flat white coffee was excellent, strong and tasty. The toasted sandwiches IO & El Clásico were delicious.... Also recommend the 'Community Cookbook" to support this fantastic charity.

title: Green Cafe
review_text:
Lovely little cafe close to Victoria Coach station where they open at 7am for breakfast which is great, wish there were more places that opened for breakfast and not brunch lol.

Had the option 3 breakfast which also included a coffee. Good quality produce and I will certainly return on future trips to London.

Staff were nice and friendly and plenty of seating inside and out.

title: Green Cafe
review_text: Our first visit to this lovely cafe, with a friendly welcome. The breakfast was hot and delicious, with good quality ingredients. Will try lunch here soon.

title: Pear Tree Cafe
address: Lakeside Cafe Battersea Park, London, SW11 4NJ
review_text: Beautiful food and good coffee everytime we visit. This is one of our favourite cafes in London for 5 years now. Always very busy but the service never disappoint. Kind and helpful staff.

title: Pear Tree Cafe
address: Lakeside Cafe Battersea Park, London, SW11 4NJ
review_text: Loved the vibes at The Pear Tree Cafe! Went for Oktoberfest and it was such a fun atmosphere - live music, great pizza, and amazing views right in the middle of Battersea Park. Perfect spot to hang out with friends and enjoy the buzz (just get there early, it gets busy!).

title: Little Portland cafe
review_text: Top 3 full English I had? Yes. Tasty buttered toasts. Big mug of coffee. Proper working class old school atmosphere, with smiling lovely staff. First time here and already made to feel like home.

title: Catch Up Cafe
address: 3 A1, Barbican Estate, Barbican, London, EC1A 4JQ
review_text: Super nice cafe with a lovely atmosphere. Asif the owner is a wonderful host and makes you feel right at home. The food was fresh and flavorful, definitely worth a visit!

title: Catch Up Cafe
address: 3 A1, Barbican Estate, Barbican, London, EC1A 4JQ
review_text:
This is one of the best cafe in London.
Located near Barbican MRT. It’s a small cafe, only 2-3 seats available inside. But most of the people just take out the coffee.
We ordered pound cake, bacon bread, and the coffee (Coffee has the fruity taste).
Cafe owner has a dog, very friendly and cute🐕.
Would like to visit again if I travel London❤️

title: Amar Cafe
review_text: I visited on a Sunday morning, and it was quite busy, however was fortunate enough to get a seat outside. Absolutely love the how this place is done, beautiful interiors and a lovely outside patio. I ordered a pour over (chose Pink Bourbon) and it was brewed to perfection at perfect temperature. Definitely visiting again.

title: Amar Cafe
review_text: One of the best coffee shops I've been to in London. Their Vegan Banana Bread is soft and very delicious. The staff are friendly and fast. It’s definitely one of the coffee shops I’ll keep coming back to every time I visit London.

title: BNB Cafe and Bar Ltd Butter Bakery Bar & Cafe
place_title: BNB Cafe and Bar Ltd Butter Bakery Bar & Cafe
review_text:
Incredibly sweet and charming people working here. We were welcomed with warmth and friendliness.
The atmosphere was relaxed and inviting, nice for three people having a late breakfast.
The food was fresh and delicious , and we left pleased with the visit.
I recommend the avocado on bread. The smoothies are also very good, ours were gone within a minute
Definitely coming back next time I’m in London!

title: Cappadocia Cafe & Bistro
review_text:
It’s a small but cozy place. We visited on a Sunday morning and it was quite busy, so we had a short wait for a table. My husband and I shared the Full English (it’s a very generous portion, and I didn't think I could manage one on my own) along with a couple of Americanos. Everything on the plate was high quality and tasted great! A fascinating and delicious contrast to the breakfasts we're used to back in Brazil.
Our server was incredibly friendly and the service was excellent (our food arrived quickly).

Highly recommend this spot if you’re anywhere near King’s Cross!

title: moko made cafe
review_text:
Such a cosy cafe. The warm honey matcha latte was so lovely, one of the best matchas i’ve had. Also tried their carrot cake with cream cheese and matcha swiss roll which were amazing, highly recommend!
A small space, so a great cafe to visit for a catch up with a friend or alone time. They have a communal table which was nice to be sat at too.
Lovely service from all the staff. Will come back!

title: Cafe OTO
review_text: One of the greatest live music venues/collective spaces in the world. Their programming is top notch and unlike any other. I have seen top notch jazz and experimental music there every time I have been. It's a very special place filled with incredible books and records to purchase as well. A must visit for me Everytime I am in London.

title: Number 1 Cafe Hackney
review_text: Hash browns were so crispy and delicious! They are generous with their portions Very homey vibe with the most welcoming and sweet staff. The owner was especially sweet and made us feel taken care of. Will have to come again when visiting London.

title: Number 1 Cafe Hackney
review_text: 1 star. As a customer I’ve had a few visits and want to share constructive feedback. On my latest visit the latte I ordered tasted weak and overly milky; the espresso flavour was almost absent. The staff were curt and not particularly helpful when taking the order, which affected the overall experience. I also noticed minor cleanliness issues around the counter area. Considering the price point, I expected better quality. I’ve attached a receipt. I’ll consider updating my rating if improvements are made.

title: Mawi Cafe
review_text:
First time visiting this café. We were tired from traveling and looking for something cozy and delicious, but unfortunately, it didn’t quite meet our expectations. The coffee was flat and not very hot. The sandwiches were average and not very tasty. Although they heated the egg and avocado sandwich, it was still served cold.
The décor of the shop was pleasing to the eye, but the chairs were not comfortable. The main reason I’m giving this place 3 stars is the staff—they were friendly and welcoming

title: Mawi Cafe
review_text: I came to Notting Hill in the search of a cute cafe to get some studying done on a sunny day (a large window and a good vibe was a must).  I spent around an hour wondering round Notting Hill, faced with tourist traps and Instagram famous cafes (no hate, but they have a time and a place....).   I was about to give up and head home until I stumbled across Mawi Cafe.  When I entered I was greeted with big smiles and a huge welcome from the friendly staff.  They recommended my the beetroot muffin which was incredible (I'm not even a big fan of chocolate muffins) and a refreshing ice americano.  I think I just loved the authenticity of the place because they weren't trying to be pretentious or instagram famous, but they seemed to just want to create a space for people to come and enjoy a part of their day.  110% recommend checking this place out.  I will note though that you can only work on the long table with a laptop - but this wasn't a problem at all!  Happy cafe hunting everyone - add this to your list! :)

title: Mawi Cafe
review_text: My sister and I went here twice during our 5 day trip to London. The banana bread and lemon bread are UNBELIEVABLE, as well as the Chai latte. This cafe is a great place to sit and read or take your coffee to go and stroll through Kensington Park which is right across the street. We loved this place!!!

title: Mawi Cafe
review_text:
We had a really good time at this cafe.
Hayat made our coffees and she was amazing. She never stopped smiling and she was really kind to us. We will definetly visit again.

title: Cafe 338 London
review_text:
Excellent dining experience for a breakfast. I've been twice in a one week visit. It's what you'd want from a full breakfast. You get a coffee or tea with your plate. A lot of good options and the plates are full and I almost can't eat it all with the toast that comes with it. I got the Full English the first time and the East End plate the second. Both good breakfast meals to start my day, and the coffee was well done, not burnt, just right. Take it black.
The service was very nice too. They were attentive, quick, and kind.
The atmosphere is perfect for a slow filling breakfast.

title: Cafe 338 London
review_text:
Excellent venue for a breakfast or brunch.

Generous portions, good value and tasty.

A wide variety of meal options and a lot of scope for customising your meal which isn’t as common in other places.

Staff are efficient and friendly.

I had the 3 pancakes with maple syrup & fruit and I added extra liver & 2 scrambled egg.

My friends had menu 1 on the pancake menu, 3 pancakes with maple syrup, ice cream & fruit and the Avocado Santa Brunch with Turkey bacon instead of bacon.

Will definitely visit again.

title: My Village Hawraman Cafe
address: 38 Chalk Farm Rd, Chalk Farm, London, NW1 8AJ
review_text:
Popped in for a drink here whilst visiting London, and were blown away.

My partner and I had a few cocktails each which were incredible, in particular the vanilla espresso martini (like tiramisu in a drink!) and the village mojito with fresh passion fruit. We were given free tzatziki and olives with our drinks which was so lovely of them to do.

We then spent our whole evening playing chess (they have games and books) and admiring the place. We will still be raving about it in Devon.

And if that wasn’t enough, the most beautiful cat was strolling around!

Can’t wait to return when we’re next visiting London.

title: My Village Hawraman Cafe
address: 38 Chalk Farm Rd, Chalk Farm, London, NW1 8AJ
review_text:
Me and the missus went here yesterday during our Camden visit to get away from the hustle and bustle of the markets, and what a bloody good decision to go here!

The atmosphere is unmatched! A gorgeous interior that feels cozy but not too claustrophobic, trinkets adorning the walls and ceilings and plants hanging from the natural light window in the back! It feels as if you have stepped in to another world and makes you forget about Camden outside! There was a selection of books to read and small items to purchase like drinks glasses which was a cool feature the missus enjoyed!

The service was wonderful, lovely staff who are unmatched with their attitude and how they make you feel so welcome. Upon ordering drinks, one order was not on the menu and they went out of their way to attempt it, and it was lovely, an iced chai latte! I had the spiced hot chocolate that is unique to the café amd beleive me I will be going back to Camden purely for that drink, it was divine!

I highly reccomend, especially for a break from the wonderful chaos that is Camden Town!

Th

title: Kozzy Cafe
review_text: Kozzy Cafe is one of my favorite spot in all of London! Great prices, wonderful staff, AMAZING portions, and yummy food all around! Worth the trip to come down here every time :)

title: Kozzy Cafe
review_text:
Wow! Four of us visited for a late brunch on a wet weekday.
Three of us had the same all-day breakfast No.3 with black pudding which included our tea and toast (note: looks like coffee is extra though).
We all agreed our meals were fantastic because:
- Portion sizes were generous.
- The quality of the ingredients and cooking was excellent. The bacon was tasty and the sausages in particular were far better quality than typical café fare.
- Staff were friendly and service as quick as could be expected for freshly-cooked food.
So, value for money was excellent.
We even heard the folks at the adjacent table saying how much they enjoyed their meal and would return.
We'll definitely be visiting Kozzy Cafe again.

title: SUNDAY cafe & restaurant
review_text:
Great little cafe for a Saturday brunch. Bit of a walk from the train, but it was worth it. We were at the head of the queue so didn’t have to wait long for a seat.

We had the courgette fritters, which had strips of panko fried courgette, with dukkah, halloumi, minted yoghurt, avocado, poached egg, harissa and Parmesan. A lot to pack in, but tasted amazing.

I had the Welsh rarebit, which takes me back to my childhood where we used to make them as snacks. This was obviously melter cheese on toast, but with bacon, fried egg, side of avocado, chilli jam, rocket and Parmesan. This was next level yum, and I’ll try to make this when I get home.

For dessert we all shared  a French toast, which was on brioche, crème fraiche, berry compote, banana and salted caramel. This was terrific, and  we all clamouring for a share of it. Delicious.

Would definitely come here again, highly recommended

title: Café Millennium
review_text: A lovely cafe. Staff are kind, warm and attentive. We stopped by for something to drink (Mocha, Tea and Latte) - which came with a Biscoff biscuit each. It was a very pleasant experience overall.

title: Café Millennium
review_text: Me, my mom and my nan were looking somewhere to eat for breakfast and my mom saw this Cafe across from where McDonald's was and it was a really delicious place and breakfast was simply amazing.

title: Café Millennium
review_text: Absolutely amazing weekend greasy breakfast spot (they also have healthy options which is sensational). Ordered the full English with extras, took minutes with food tasking fresh and piping hot. Large variety of items and the service feels homely and caring. Would highly recommend if your in the area

title: Café Millennium
review_text:
First time visit and Eni was just lovely and food was just fantastic fresh and great portion size.

Just lovely service and friendliness was just brilliant from all staff. Eni obviously take pride in the service she provides.

title: Café Millennium
review_text: Amazing service, great staff, super friendly. Feels like home. Me an my family come here for the amazing breakfast they make. Highly recommended. Keep up the great work guys x

title: Café Millennium
review_text: First time vist. Good range of food. I only had set 4.

title: Café Millennium
review_text: The food was good, service was a little shaky buy ok. It was nice and quiet inside and clean and tidy. I had the set menu 6 with tea and was very nice.

title: Rose Cafe
review_text: What a great little Cafe. Down in London for the day and decided to come early to get a breakfast here (not my first time). Great food, huge portion, great value (especially for London). Strong recommend.

title: Alchemy Cafe - Spitalfields
review_text: One of the best coffees in the area.... And there's a lot of great coffees around! The staff know their stuff. It's a bit hidden in the lobby of a company building but it is accessible to the public. Worth a detour to have an incredible coffee from here. Both the Espresso and the filter drinks are v good. It's not your usual coffee shop vibe but nice quiet lobby in the heart of Spitalfields.

title: The Electric Elephant Cafe
review_text: First time in this cafe and definitely won’t be the last. Was greeted warmly by a lovely lady who instantly made me feel welcome, had the most delicious veggie breakfast and great coffee. Would definitely recommend!

title: The Travel Cafe
review_text: We booked the terrarium workshop and would highly recommend. She was lovely and very knowledgeable and helped us create unique terrariums.

title: The Travel Cafe
review_text: Thanks Heyoung for a lovely terrarium making experience. So pleased with my creation and looking forward to seeing it evolve. The travel cafe is full of beautiful plants and has a lovely calm feel.  A well spent couple of hours.. it was so easy to forget it was the hottest day of the year.

title: The Travel Cafe
review_text:
Very quaint grab and go cafe with lots of plants and cosy atmosphere.

The coffee is very nice Ozone coffee. I truly enjoyed my latte, and loved browsing the plants inside the store while waiting for the barista to make my drink.

Unfortunately there is no options to sit inside, I believe this changed after Covid and is now a take out cafe.

I would have loved to see some designs on the cup-sleeve to put it to the next level. This is not a negative, but a kind suggestion.

title: The Travel Cafe
review_text: Cute hip little cafe, very stylish with lovely staff and tasty food. Not suuper cheap but perfectly worth it. Also cater for vegan and gluten free.

title: The Travel Cafe
review_text:
The cafe looks nice from the outside and also very attractive on the inside, due to the plants and flowers, which are for sale.

Despite the name, the cafe is clearly an add on though given the erratic opening hours.  Weirdly they close at 4pm most days and at 2pm on a weekend. Frustrating and off putting so we won't be attempting to visit again.

There are plenty of very decent cafes on Lower Marsh just across the road. We took our business to one of these independents - one of which serves award winning coffee.

title: The Travel Cafe
review_text: terrarium experience was amazing we had a great time creating it, calming and relaxing time with Hayoung being a lovely guide.

title: The Travel Cafe
review_text: Rented the back room private meeting space and it was great! TV with HDMI to cast contents from laptop, staff were super friendly. Drinks included were fantastic, especially the lattes. The cafe itself (front end) is now more of a plant store.

title: The Travel Cafe
review_text: Serene, relaxed and cosy. This is the only cafe in the area I will go in, order a coffee, and simply sit on my own for twenty minutes to drink it. With orchestral music in the background and surrounded by plants, it's a great escape from the outside. Coffee is lovely and staff are unwaveringly polite and welcoming in a quiet and gentle way. This isn't a hipster trap, it's a very human and easy space to spend a time. Oh and on the plant note, they're very knowledgeable and it's a great place to procure that plant you need for your bedroom.

title: Drupe Cafe • Broadway Market
review_text:
This place lives and breathes great food, culture and community!

The interior is rustic, modern and slight Mediterranean vibes with beautiful flooring throughout. Food looks so fresh and beautiful brassy elements at the bar.

Crowd here is very 'indie' East London and some families.

I ordered the pancakes which came with cream, hit of rose and pistachios. Absolutely amazing. Loved the Turkish touch. Also had a few coffees that were great and a vegan chocolate avocado cake.

Worth a visit if you're looking for something more light and tasty with a twist.

title: Mess Cafe
review_text:
We loved 'Mess Cafe', just the place we were seeking for breakfast during our visit to Hackney.  We all agreed that the breakfasts were delicious, generous, well cooked & presented with excellent service & fairly priced. The staff were friendly & efficient. A nice atmosphere & lovely interior, a well deserved 10/10. We'll definately return.

(A caveat, 'Mess Cafe' serves Halal options & I need to make clear as a warning to others but there's plenty of options to avoid it!)

title: Love Walk Cafe
review_text:
Best eggs Benny in town!! Unbelievable hollandaise sauce. This is my favorite place easily in Fulham. I come all the time, including for my birthday.

Everyone who works here is so lovely!

Thank you thank you!!

A must visit, great prices. I never have to wait to get a spot, plenty of outdoor and a decent amount of indoor seating.

title: Mare Street Cafe Bar
review_text: First time visit and we were impressed with the friendly service and good food. My mother-in-law and I commented on how clean the cafe was which shows the staff's care and pride in their business. Will visit again.

title: Meral’s Cafe Bistro
review_text:
A friend recommended this place in the heart of Cricklewood, and from the moment I walked in, I immediately felt comfortable. The atmosphere is calm yet functional, and the décor is warm and welcoming. The staff are genuinely kind and cheerful, even very early in the morning.

They open at 6:30 am, which makes it a convenient morning pit stop for anyone commuting into central London via Thameslink. There’s a wide selection of English breakfast options, sweet and savoury, and you can even build your own plate by choosing exactly what you want. It’s the first time I’ve seen this level of flexibility, and it gives the place a very homely feel.

The food quality is great, and a special highlight is the coffee. They serve Kimbo coffee from Naples, one of Italy’s most respected brands, which easily ranks among the best coffees you can find. Compliments to the owners and staff for creating such a welcoming, efficient, and friendly place

title: Meral’s Cafe Bistro
review_text: I came here for breakfast during my trip to London and enjoyed it very much. The eggs Benedict and the pancaked are recommendable, as well as the hot chocolate. I only visited twice, but would have loved to come more often. Especially because the host is so nice and welcoming. We talked a bit and he recommended that I visit Kew gardens, which were just lovely. Thank you so much again! The café is very cozy, if a bit loud. But that just adds more soul to it if you ask me. I felt safe and left well fed and happy for another exciting day in London.

title: Meral’s Cafe Bistro
review_text: This is the first time that I have used this cafe and I would definitely come back. I opted for the Merals vegetarian breakfast andcI was not disappointed with the quality of the food and the portion size.  The staff there were really friendly and made you feel really welcome

title: Meral’s Cafe Bistro
review_text:
I’m from Canada and discovered this place two years ago while visiting London with my girlfriend.

This bistro was close to our hotel and was our go-to for the breakfast.

The food is affordable and very tasty, with a nice presentation and everything.

The interior is gorgeous and is a very nice place to sit in. I keep a very good memory of this place.

I would 100% recommend to anyone passing through the area.

title: Hamlet Cafe & Restaurant
review_text:
I love breakfast as anyone will know - if they have read any of my reviews. So I'm on the hunt for the best English breakfast or variety of cafe breakfasts . I'm in Hamlets on Hornsey rd .  I had their Ophelia breakfast (changing veg sausages to Cumberland)
This has become one of my top breakfasts.
My friend has a cheese and mushroom omelette - see the photos ( yes I eat mine 🤤)
Go out of your way to visit this Cafe IT is up there.
Happy breakfast

title: Hard Rock Cafe
review_text:
We were genuinely surprised by how fresh, tasty and beautifully presented the food was at Hard Rock Cafe London. We went for the classics — the Legendary Burger and the absolutely massive Brownie Sundae that everyone talks about. And yes… both dishes fully lived up to the hype. The burger was juicy and perfectly balanced, and the sundae was a dessert you simply must try at least once in your life — rich, generous and pure happiness in a bowl. But what truly made our visit unforgettable was our waiter, Florin.
Professional, knowledgeable and incredibly attentive, he made us feel like true “musical royalty.” From sharing fun stories about the memorabilia to making sure our experience was perfect from start to finish, he added an extra sparkle to our first Hard Rock moment in London. A delicious meal, amazing service, and a bit of rock ’n’ roll magic — the perfect combo.

title: Hard Rock Cafe
review_text:
4077 / Eleanor 💜
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ An unforgettable experience at the Hard Rock Café Piccadilly Circus!
What an amazing visit! From the moment you walk in, you can feel the unique atmosphere – vibrant, welcoming and absolutely iconic. The whole team is super friendly, attentive and full of positive energy – you instantly feel at home, as if you’re among friends.

The food was simply sensational – juicy burgers cooked to perfection, crispy fries, and a dessert you’ll dream about long after. Everything was fresh, flavourful and served with that signature Hard Rock flair – loud, lively and full of character!

A huge thank you to the fantastic team who made this visit such a memorable one. Everything was spot on – service, vibe and food all on world-class level! 🤘🍔🎸

Highly recommended – 10/10, I’ll definitely be back!

title: North Lodge Café
address: Brompton Cemetery, North Lodge, Lillie Rd, London SW5 9JE
review_text: The atmosphere was nice, and the staff were very friendly. I tried a flat white, although I prefer lactose-free milk. They had alternatives, so I opted for a flat white with almond milk, which was tasty. The blueberry muffin was delicious as well.

title: North Lodge Café
address: Brompton Cemetery, North Lodge, Lillie Rd, London SW5 9JE
review_text:
really bright and wonderful place with great & clean facilities. staff working there today were super nice and very welcoming, something which is not always the case when going to cafes.
have a little stroll in the beautiful grounds of Brompton cemetery then get some food & drink here while you chat about your walk, you won’t be disappointed.

title: North Lodge Café
address: Brompton Cemetery, North Lodge, Lillie Rd, London SW5 9JE
review_text:
Nice environment. Especially enjoyable to sit outside when the sunshine is nice.

Coffee is not overpriced, but the quality is really bad. Ordered a latte and it tastes like I’m drinking grounded bark….
Not sure about their food.

title: North Lodge Café
address: Brompton Cemetery, North Lodge, Lillie Rd, London SW5 9JE
review_text: Lovely little Cafe just inside the the main gate of Brompton Cemetery. Lots of table to sit and, with outside seating as well. Good choice of small breakfasts time, soft drinks and some cold food options. Make a great coffee as well, with dairy free mild options. Friendly staff.

title: North Lodge Café
address: Brompton Cemetery, North Lodge, Lillie Rd, London SW5 9JE
review_text: An excellent cafe inside the grounds of Brompton Cemetery, however the seating is quite limited. There were free packs of coffee grounds to take home.

title: North Lodge Café
address: Brompton Cemetery, North Lodge, Lillie Rd, London SW5 9JE
review_text:
Coffee with a view of an old iconic cemetery. We had a peaceful, warm April day and a long conversation about life.
The Café is decent, in unusual setting, with plenty of seating, but coffee itself could be better: lattes are tiny, with too much froth.

title: North Lodge Café
address: Brompton Cemetery, North Lodge, Lillie Rd, London SW5 9JE
review_text: Nice little cafe at one of the cemetery entrances. Coffee is good. The usual range of snacks. Some cakes. It's ok. Functional

title: Golders Hill Park Cafe
address: Golders Hill, N End Way, London, NW3 7HD
review_text: Good cafe with decent food, plenty of options to choose from, including kids menu. One of the signs says this cafe has been around for more than 50 years! There was a bit of queue when we visited but it moved quickly. Plenty of seating inside and outside. Decent ice cream shop for after-meal-sweetness.

title: Golders Hill Park Cafe
address: Golders Hill, N End Way, London, NW3 7HD
review_text: Visited this cafe for the first time.  The cappuccino is truly disappointing.  Like second time brew without much flavour and texture.  The tomato & cheese croissant is nice though.

title: Golders Hill Park Cafe
address: Golders Hill, N End Way, London, NW3 7HD
review_text: Amazing lunch spot! We love the selection of cakes and salmon is great. Have beautiful terrace. Next to playground.  Very popular cafe!! Must to visit highly recommend

title: Polo Bar - 24 Hour Great British Cafe
place_title: Polo Bar - 24 Hour Great British Cafe
review_text:
Visited on Sunday, 08th February 2026, at 1630. First time visit and very quiet at this time. We were meeting another family member coming in from Essex, whilst we came from West London, so the cafe was convenient as well as offering a fun birthday experience on a Sunday in this part of town, which is pretty quiet and closed off normally. Small cafe spread over 3 floors. We were directed to the ground-floor seating area, but the seating on the two upper floors is nicer.

Good menu focusing on British staples and being open 24 hours a day, there were only a few items not available at all times. Quick service and the food was excellent, finished off by a very good value and fun birthday quadruple pancake & red velvet cake combo, which was presented with a round of Happy Birthday from the staff too.

£100 for 4 of us inc automatically added service charge of 10%, as it should be, rather than the higher percentage added by other establishments recently. I would happily return if in the area, though I probably wouldn't bother booking.

title: Polo Bar - 24 Hour Great British Cafe
place_title: Polo Bar - 24 Hour Great British Cafe
review_text:
We travel over 300 miles to eat in this diner and travel by train and have done for the last 8 years so was really disappointed with what happened today.  The waitress that took our order she was really nice, pleasant and helpful the issue only arose when the food turned up .
I ordered the traditional breakfast which  The tall skinny male host insisted it was the Spanish breakfast!!!. My colleague had a burger and fries.
With my plate the Beans, mushrooms and sausage were cold and the rest of the plate was tepid.  My colleague who had the burger said his chips were cold and the bacon in his bun was burnt to a crisp
We didn’t say anything at the time because when you order hot food you expect it to be hot, you shouldn’t have to get people’s attention to alert them to the fault which should have been check prior to serving.

We finished what we could and left spending less time in the restaurant than it did to travel there.

Due to the experience we are cancelling our customer staff party, family reunion late next year.
We can’t take the risk with staff members and especially family members eating cold food and or food not being checked and possibly the risk of illness.

title: The V&A Cafe
review_text:
Went here after seeing travel videos featuring it on YouTube.

The architecture truly is amazing. There's the main room in the middle that's the most beautiful one and 2 more on either side of it that are nice, but not as impressive.

That being the case, the main room is packed and the only open seating we found was in one of the less pretty rooms off to the side.

It's nice that you don't have to go through the entire V&A museum to get access to the cafe, although we did go through a small part of the Buddhist section. I liked that, though, because we still got a taste of the museum.

We came around 1 hour to closing, 4pm, so there weren't long lines to get our food.

It's cafeteria style and the 2 stalls I saw facing each other were selling the same thing.

We got to try scones for the first time which I liked better than the mango & raspberry cake I also ordered which was too sweet.

The prices are reasonable. For 2 desserts and 2 drinks between 2 people it came to around $22 total, so around $11 each.

Even the bathroom was nice, but I noticed they made the majority of the stalls and sinks available to the public be the more modern ones while the really pretty old fashioned one only had a few stalls.

Afterwards, we wandered into the central courtyard and admired the architecture.

Overall, it was a good experience. Just don't expect to sit in the really pretty main room, which is why I went there in the first place and I'm sure many other people do, too.

title: The V&A Cafe
review_text:
I visited the café inside the V&A Museum and was genuinely stunned by its beautiful interior. The colourful ceramic tiles, detailed patterns, sculptures, and stained-glass windows create an elegant and historic atmosphere — it really is a stunning space to sit in.

I ordered a small pot of Earl Grey tea and a carrot and walnut cake, which were served on a paper plate with temporary cutlery, and the total came to £9.70. For that price, I believe I could have had better quality food and a nicer dining experience in a hotel.

The café was also extremely crowded and noisy, and the food felt overpriced for what it was. Because of that, some of the charm was lost, and it felt more like a commercial operation than a relaxing museum café.

It’s still a beautiful spot to take a short break after exploring the V&A, but I wouldn’t come here specifically for the food.

title: The V&A Cafe
review_text: What a beautiful place! The food selection is quite good, but very much overpriced. Basic service. OK coffee. But it is perfect for a little break during your visit. Stunning decor.

title: The V&A Cafe
review_text:
A café in London with the rudest staff ever.
I visited around 4 p.m. today with my baby.
I ordered a scone and a latte, and since I thought I wouldn’t be able to finish the scone, I asked for a takeaway box.
But the staff said they don’t provide that. When I told her that I was given one the last time I visited, she threw the box and cream at me, and even ignored my “thank you.”
She was a short woman who looked Indian. She was incredibly rude and unpleasant.

And while I was sitting and drinking my coffee, an older male staff member asked me if I could move for another customer who needed to charge their laptop. I was honestly speechless—he came to me multiple times.
The customer even said it was fine and that they’d come later, but for some reason he kept bothering me. Maybe he thought I was an easy target because I’m Asian.

It really feels like this place only hires strange, unprofessional people. No class at all.

title: Springfield Park Cafe
review_text:
Springfield Park Café — Serenity with a Brew

Located at the top of Springfield Park in Hackney, this café is housed in an 18th-century manor that offers a light, airy atmosphere and panoramic views of the park and River Lea. It’s a go-to spot for those seeking tranquility, nature, and a well-made cup of coffee.

Coffee & treats:

Strong, aromatic coffee — perfect for a morning boost or afternoon pause
Herbal teas and fresh juices — light and refreshing options
Homemade cakes and cookies — often vegan or gluten-free
Simple lunch plates — including Mediterranean platters and vegan breakfasts

Atmosphere:

Quiet and scenic — ideal for solo visits, reading, or reflection
Outdoor seating — with views of the park and canal
Friendly staff — known for warm service and quick turnaround
Dog-friendly and inclusive — welcoming to all visitors

title: Springfield Park Cafe
review_text: After visiting today, I can amend my review. The grilled breakfast was beautiful. Not sure what has changed but I hope it continues. Well done! My friends chicken salad was very disappointing however.  Her chicken was very hard and over done. My other friend thoroughly enjoyed her vegiie breakfast. My review is based on what I had today. I recommend the breakfasts. And setting is a plus. Sorry, I was half way through eating when I remembered to take a pic.

title: Bonnington Cafe and Community Centre
review_text: Attended an art opening combined with birthday party and Syrian cook / chef dished up the most flavoursome food. Ottolenghi without the price tag. Usually cooks Friday eves. Defo worth a visit folks!

title: Bonnington Cafe and Community Centre
review_text:
Whatever day of the week you visit the Bonnington Cafe you won't regret it - it is an incredible place to eat and one of my favourite veggie spots in the whole world. Dining spaces are so cosy and homely it feels like visiting friends and family, and the value is unbeatable.

If you can, visit on a Wednesday to meet Arfa and enjoy her delicious food, warm hospitality and generous portions - we ate until we couldn't eat any more and took some home! Thank you Arfa, we'll be back (often hopefully!)

title: Cafe in the Courtyard (Seasonal - summer)
address: St Martin-in-the-Fields, Trafalgar Sq, London, WC2N 4JH
review_text: The staff of just two worked their socks off trying to serve and cook for the vast number of visitors who were obviously unexpected for that day. Beautiful outdoor seating and a view of the gardens.

title: Thenga Cafe
review_text: Lovely vegetarian cafe near kings cross station. Handy for lunch if working at / visiting the British Library

title: Lantana Fitzrovia
review_text:
Highly recommend the Pork Belly Benedict (weekend brunch menu) and Crispy Chicken French toast. Coffee is great. Please note: they automatically charge for tap water and apparently donate it to a charity. We asked for this to be removed as we already have other charities we are supporting.

We also came early on a Sunday (10:30am)  and managed to get a seat for 2 without a booking but could only stay an hour (this was perfect for us).

title: Lantana Fitzrovia
review_text:
THIS PLACE IS AMAZING
They serve amazing brunch! Every one of us (6 people) had an amazing experience.
Every dish was so good and well thought out! The service was so kind and with grace. Every single one of the employees made us feel so welcome.
Coffee was excellent.
You must go there.

title: Lantana Fitzrovia
review_text:
An excellent cafe that usually NEEDS RESERVATION. It's good and the people know it. Food is an interesting global bland of flavours, rooted cuisines but drawing flavours, spices, ideas from other. And done very well at that. Had the pork belly eggs Benedict, which was light than expect but a really nice blend of sirrach in it. The show stopper for me me was the chicken french toast with chili maple syrup, the sweet savoury blend was beautifully done and balanced excellently, a dish you could eat quite a bit of at a go. Sorry for the photos, got too excited and forget about it.

Good coffee and a dirty good chai latte. And we got a birthday dessert too! Thank you 😊 for a photo for this one at least

title: Lantana Fitzrovia
review_text: We made a reservation for breakfast.  When we arrived they were just opening and it was quiet and we weren’t sure the reservation was necessary.  But within 30 minutes the place was full and we were glad we had one.  There were a lot of people who appeared to be repeat customers - which speaks volumes.  Our service was quick and staff were kind and welcoming.  I had the French toast for breakfast.  I underestimated the level of spice that would be in a French toast dish - so it was a little on the hot side for me.  But it was all well prepared and beautifully presented.

title: Lantana Fitzrovia
review_text: Amazing coffee! Great gluten free food- amazing halloumi smashed avocado. Absolutely delicious tomato chutney ontop, would love to go back

title: Lantana Fitzrovia
review_text: Great side street coffee shop with seating for served food. The food was good and had a lovely atmosphere.

title: Lantana Fitzrovia
review_text: Just had brunch at Lantana, very busy so book in advance. Sat outside on a sunny autumn Sunday. Great flat white and OJ, smoked salmon, avocado, poached egg on sourdough, with a twist of courgette and perfectly grilled and lightly spiced tomatoes. Service was friendly and pretty quick considering how popular/busy it was. Strongly recommend !

title: Lantana Fitzrovia
review_text:
We came here for breakfast and both enjoyed our meals. I got the crispy French toast, I was slightly worried the chilli syrup might be too spicy for me but it was good.
We made a reservation but we got there for opening so not sure it was required. It got busier as we were eating. The staff were friendly and efficient.
The toilet is downstairs.

title: Clifton Nurseries Cafe
address: Clifton Nursery Yard, 5A Clifton Villas, London, W9 2PH
review_text:
🌿 Five Stars for The Quince Tree Café/Nursery; A Botanical Dream with Culinary Brilliance

Stepping into The Quince Tree Café/Nursery feels like entering a secret garden curated by aesthetes and epicureans alike. Nestled within a lush nursery, this London gem offers a dining experience that is as visually enchanting as it is gastronomically refined.

From the moment you arrive, you’re enveloped in a serene ambiance, sunlight filtering through glass ceilings, verdant foliage cascading from above, and classical architectural touches that whisper elegance. The setting alone is a masterclass in atmosphere: airy, romantic, and effortlessly chic.

The menu is a celebration of seasonal British produce, plated with finesse and served with warmth. Poached eggs arrive like soft clouds atop artisan toast, kissed with microgreens and a drizzle of golden olive oil. Each dish is thoughtfully composed, vibrant, fresh, and indulgent without excess. Even the simplest elements, like heirloom tomatoes or a ramekin of balsamic, are elevated to art.

Service is gracious and intuitive, striking the perfect balance between attentiveness and discretion. Whether you’re sipping champagne in the garden or savoring a leisurely brunch beneath the vines, every moment feels curated for delight.

The Quince Tree Café/Nursery is more than a meal, it’s a sensory retreat, a love letter to nature and nourishment. A must-visit for those who crave beauty with their breakfast and refinement with their rosé.

title: Clifton Nurseries Cafe
address: Clifton Nursery Yard, 5A Clifton Villas, London, W9 2PH
review_text:
Clifton Nurseries Cafe is an absolute hidden gem in the heart of the city! Tucked away within the beautiful surroundings of the Clifton Nurseries, this cafe feels like a peaceful escape from the hustle and bustle. It has a charming and cozy atmosphere, perfect for relaxing with friends or even spending some quiet time on your own. The moment you walk in, you’re greeted by incredibly friendly staff who go out of their way to make you feel welcome. The service is always warm and attentive, which really enhances the overall experience.

The menu offers a great selection of delicious food, whether you're after a light snack or a more substantial meal. Everything I’ve tried has been fresh and tasty, with lovely flavors that really hit the spot. Their coffee is also fantastic—rich, smooth, and expertly brewed. It’s the perfect pick-me-up, whether you’re starting your day or just taking a break in the afternoon.

Another plus is that they offer free Wi-Fi, which makes it a great spot for getting some work done, catching up on emails, or simply enjoying a leisurely morning with a coffee and your laptop. The setting is serene and inspiring, which makes it easy to get lost in the peaceful ambiance.

If you’re in London and looking for a tranquil hideaway with great food, excellent coffee, and wonderful service, Clifton Nurseries Cafe is an absolute must-visit. Highly recommend spending some time here—you won’t be disappointed!

title: Clifton Nurseries Cafe
address: Clifton Nursery Yard, 5A Clifton Villas, London, W9 2PH
review_text:
⸻

🌿 5 stars

I visited Clifton Nurseries today with family and friends to pick out some plants for my home — and it turned out to be one of the loveliest starts to the weekend I can remember. The weather was beautiful, the setting was peaceful and full of charm, and we enjoyed absolutely delicious scones with cream and coffee at the café.

It honestly felt like a little slice of heaven right in London. To top it all off, I managed to capture what might be the best photo ever taken of my mum — a memory I’ll treasure.

A truly special place. Highly recommend for a relaxed day out, whether you’re a plant lover or just in search of a calm, uplifting spot.

title: Criterion Cafe
review_text: As tourists who are coming for the first time just to try the English breakfast, I think their service was exceptional!

title: Leafwild Cafe
review_text:
Located just of the busy touristic Portobello Road, this cafe offers you everything you could look for when grabbing a coffee.

A great atmosphere to work in (especially by the window), amazing coffee, good vibes, great home-made like food and the best part is, all the baristas are so lovely and friendly. They take time to ask how you are and make sure your trip to the cafe was worth it. They are very good at accommodating to your food and drink needs too!

Couldn’t recommend this cafe more.

title: Whiskers & Cream Cat Cafe London
review_text:
My daughter and I visited today for a pre-booked study session……safe to say, we did not get much studying done at all!

The cats were just gorgeous. They were friendly and full of personality and certainly know how to captivate an audience.

The staff on duty today were fantastic. They were attentive, friendly and the atmosphere was beautifully relaxed - a fellow attendee was celebrating her birthday today and had treated herself to a treat by attending, staff on duty presented a slice of cake with a candle in it as a surprise - little touches like this made the experience shine! We will be returning in the not too distant future - This message to the kind stranger celebrating her birthday, thank you for your advice x

title: Whiskers & Cream Cat Cafe London
review_text: First visit. Very welcoming. The cats live here and have places to go if they don't want to interact but they all seem very happy for attention. It's evident they are well cared for. Staff ensure they are respected. The cappuccino came with a cute cat pattern decoration and the lemon drizzle cake was delicious. A lovely visit and we will be back!

title: Whiskers & Cream Cat Cafe London
review_text: I went to Whiskers & Cream on Sunday afternoon. I was greeted by the loveliest hostess. They didn’t have space immediately, but said to come back in an hour. I came back an hour later and wow was it worth it! To start with, the customer service was next to none. The staff are so warm and welcoming. I arrived at snoozing time so the cats were all having their afternoon naps. However, I was more than content to sit and watch them doze away. I was lucky enough to have Jack the Maine Coon hop up on the seat next to me and snooze for the entire time I was there. What a gorgeous boy! It’s very evident how happy and comfortable the cats are, and the care for them is second to none. I paid £18 for 1.5 hour peak session which included a hot drink. I had a hot chocolate, which was fab! Given that all this goes back into cat care, staff wages and the betterment of the cat cafe, I was more than willing to pay this. Thank you Whiskers & Cream for such a delightful afternoon, I can’t wait to visit again soon.

title: Cafe Route - Dalston
address: Gaumont Tower, Dalston Square, London, E8 3BQ
review_text:
Great brunch spot in Dalston — perfect for work, chill, and a surprisingly good peach salad!

Discovered this Cafe Route location in Dalston Square and it quickly became one of my go-to spots for a relaxed brunch or a casual work session. The space is bright and calm, with plenty of tables (some with sockets!) so you’ll often see people working or studying here — great atmosphere for it.

I tried the peach salad for the first time and it was honestly amazing! Super refreshing, with a lovely balance of sweet and tangy, paired with cheese and toasted nuts. It gave off a light Mediterranean vibe and was the perfect summer pick.

title: Cafe Route - Dalston
address: Gaumont Tower, Dalston Square, London, E8 3BQ
review_text:
Worthy Brunch Spot Behind Dalston Station!

After exploring Columbia Road Flower Market, I stumbled upon this super Instagrammable and laid-back brunch spot right behind Dalston Station. The space is bright, airy, and flooded with perfect lighting—ideal for snapping those aesthetic foodie shots! 📸

Drinks:Tried the *Peachy Green* fresh juice—a blend of kale, peach, and greens. Loved it! A vitamin-packed, fiber-rich choice (totally influencer-approved 💁).

Main Dish:The *Mediterranean Plate* was HUGE and hit all the right notes. Highlights:
- Spicy olives for a tangy kick.
- Crusty artisan bread slathered with honey and clotted cream (a match made in heaven!).
- A protein trio: perfectly runny fried egg, savory Turkish sausage, and roasted veggies drizzled with their signature zesty sauce.

Sat by the window soaking up the sun—such a blissful brunch moment! ☀️ The menu also has gorgeous options like yogurt bowls and shakshuka. Can’t wait to come back to try them next time.

Verdict:No regrets here—flavors, vibes, and portions all on point. A must-visit for brunch lovers!

title: Matchbox Cafe
address: 1A Elm Ter, Constantine Rd, London, NW3 2LL
review_text: First time and very welcomed.  Did not order food but the coffee was a good size, reasonable price and the service was excellent!  Will definitely visit again if in the area to have a bite to eat to support this cafe!

title: Matchbox Cafe
address: 1A Elm Ter, Constantine Rd, London, NW3 2LL
review_text: A lovely place! Sandwiches were absolutely brilliant and so were the hot chocolates. Lovely staff too! Well priced also. Worth a visit for amazing sandwiches and paints.

title: John Lewis Cafe Bar
review_text:
This café and restaurant offers a perfect balance of quality over presentation, prioritizing flavor and freshness over flashy appearances. It follows an open-service concept—grab a tray, explore a variety of well-prepared options, and pay at the counter. The experience is casual yet refined, making it ideal for both a quick bite and a leisurely break.

The venue features a variety of seating areas, including a charming outdoor terrace overlooking bustling Oxford Street, providing a peaceful escape with a view. Located on the fifth floor of the John Lewis building, the café is easily accessible and well-situated for shoppers and visitors alike. I highly recommend giving it a try—it’s a delightful spot that combines convenience, quality, and atmosphere in one memorable stop.

title: Lumberjack Cafe
review_text: First time going here after years of walking by and it’s so much more spacious than meets the eye from the outside. There’s a great seating area downstairs great for doing work or having meetings. I can imagine the summer time with the garden being accessible too. Has a slight rustic and laid back feel to the environment. The Matcha Latte and Biscoff Cake was amazing too! S/O to both Neoma and Daisy for being so welcoming and friendly staff. Both professional and pleasant. 100% recommend!

title: Kensington Palace Cafe
review_text:
The atmosphere gets a 5 because its a Wednesday in Decmeber and no-body is here. Wooop! I imagine a July weekend may be slightly less pleasant.

So, I was pleasantly surprised by this place, i'm in a palace. After getting ravagely attacked by a squirrel in the park, I felt it was a time for a break and a brew and when I looked up, I saw this big building dead ahead, with a cafe attached to the side!
so I decided to pop in, see what its all about. After the security check where they confiscated my revolver and anthetamins, I was allowed to visit the cafe!

So the place itself? Well I think this is where Kate and Wills get up to all sorts of tomfoolery and yet im allowed to have a sausage roll here, nice!
And not only that, its was a good sausage roll, and the prices are reasonable (for London).
I've also had 2 mince pies and a nice cuppa.
What more can a man ask for?

After I've downed my tea, I may even buy something from the gift shop. Who knows, maybe they have walking sticks to help me fend of the vicious rodents in the park?

title: Kensington Palace Cafe
review_text: Nice café with a lovely outdoor space if you don't mind the bees. Service was friendly and food was nice. We had cream tea (scone, clotted cream, strawberry jam and pot of tea for £7.50) and a Victoria sponge cake slice.

title: Kensington Palace Cafe
review_text: Quiche overdone on top, fine I can get over that, although portion doesn't match the price. But the chicken ciabatta was next level poor, bottom was soggy, top was rock hard couldn't even chew it, the rocket was wilted and the chicken didn't even look like chicken. Found it strange that the buns/cakes were out where people were lining up people breathing on them as they waited in line. Scone from behind the counter was fine but only just.

title: Kensington Palace Cafe
review_text: The catering team is so good at what they do (wonder if it’s the same caters to the royal family), chocolate cake was moist, fluffy, rich and delicious! Despite being quite busy the staff were always hospitable and patiently explained allergies. 10/10

title: Kensington Palace Cafe
review_text:
The cafe has a Gluten Free wrap onna separate platter with a separate colored tong.
It was zesty and tasty and a good size, and I enjoyed it. I did not have a reaction from it.

The reason I didn’t give 5 stars is the trays are tiny and they should have a tray return like in Germany because pigeons were swarming the leftovers on another table that had yet to be bused.

title: Kensington Palace Cafe
