review_text: I stopped by Kensington Palace Café after touring the palace and gardens — it was the perfect place to relax. The setting is calm with both indoor and outdoor seating. Staff were friendly and efficient, even when it got busy. I loved the fruit scone with clotted cream and jam — fresh, moist, and full of flavor. There’s a good range of teas, coffees, and light bites, with options for vegetarians and vegans. Prices felt reasonable for the area. A great little stop right by the palace — I’ll definitely return!

title: Kensington Palace Cafe
review_text:
I’ll definitely return for afternoon tea at some point, and for the pineapple mousse cake of the size of a pineapple circular slice, which was delicious obviously! (I didn’t take a picture, so I’ve attempted a GenAI model of it, but the real was better!)

The place is delightful, especially in the outdoors gardens, and a perfect location  for summer chats and tea, cold press juices, ice creams (none of which I’ve tried, but I will next time!), lemon and Victoria sponge cakes.

The store is heart-lifting too! With cute books, Palace souvenirs, lovely garments.

title: Kensington Palace Cafe
review_text:
Cafe was nice, the food was tasty and the service was nice and helpful.
However, the cafe did feel a bit basic and it was a bit busy, not just because of the cafe but because it is situated right next to the gift shop displays, and it seemed the visitors were just here for the gift shop and not the cafe, even though the crowd would die down after we settled down and started eating lunch.

title: The Pavilion Cafe
address: road access via, Muswell Hill Rd, Onslow Gardens, London N10 3JN
review_text: Lovely little secluded cafe hidden away in the woods. Very English cottage/countryside feel. Prices are a little expensive but it's understandable for the area. Service was great, friendly and efficient. I believe it was the cafe owner who served us - he was very generous by offering complimentary additions! First visit to the cafe and this level of service has left a very good impression!  Would highly recommend! Will happily visit again. Nice ample outdoor seating - we enjoyed a little rest here in the sunshine and carried on with our walk in the woods.

title: Cafe Diana
review_text:
Cafe Diana is truly one of the most unique places you can visit in Notting Hill. It’s more than just a café or restaurant — it feels like a living tribute to Diana, Princess of Wales.

The walls are completely covered with an incredible collection of photographs and memorabilia — beautiful, framed pictures of Diana from different moments of her life around the world. The collection is immense. Just sitting there and looking at the photos before your food even arrives puts a smile on your face. It feels warm, nostalgic, and welcoming.

The atmosphere is always lively and diverse. There’s a real mix of people from all over the world, and it’s especially family-friendly — you’ll often see families and children enjoying their meals, which says a lot about the place.

The food is absolutely delicious — proper home-style cooking with generous portions. I had rice, pasta, salad, kebab, and yogurt, and everything was full of flavour. The staff go above and beyond to make you feel at home. On my recent visit, the owner personally came around and kindly offered everyone a complimentary bowl of soup, which was such a thoughtful touch.

If you love Diana, are visiting the UK, or simply want comforting food with a great vibe in the heart of the city, Cafe Diana is definitely worth visiting. Anytime I’m in Notting Hill, it’s a place I’m happy to return to.

Highly recommended.

title: Cafe Diana
review_text:
More Than Just a Cafe – A Beautiful Tribute! We chose Cafe Diana for its unbeatable location near the consulate and were so pleasantly surprised. Stepping inside is like stepping into a museum of beautiful memories dedicated to Princess Diana. The walls are adorned with photographs and tributes, making it a truly special place to visit.
We were on a tight schedule but were served quickly without feeling rushed. The food was delicious, generously portioned, and looked exactly like the photos—which isn't always the case! Considering its location in Kensington, the prices were very reasonable.

It’s the perfect spot for a convenient, quality bite to eat, especially if you have business in the area. The heartfelt homage to the People's Princess makes it a memorable experience rather than just a meal.

title: Cafe Diana
review_text:
We found a little gem in Kensington — Diana’s Café. The walls are full of beautiful photographs of Princess Diana, including touching images of her with young William and Harry. It adds a warm, personal feel to the space — like a quiet tribute to the People’s Princess right in her old neighbourhood.

We ordered freshly squeezed orange juice (a must!) and it didn’t disappoint — perfectly sweet, ice-cold, and clearly pressed on the spot.

For a moment of calm, a dose of nostalgia, and a truly royal start to your day — Diana’s Café is well worth a visit.

title: Caffè Nero
address: 1a Central Saint Giles Piazza, St Giles High St, London, WC2H 8AG
review_text:
Very spacious and comfortable, with air conditioning that was a true blessing in the summer heat. The café’s location couldn’t be more convenient and it is just a short walk from the station.

I had amazing service from Hajrie, who was incredibly sweet and welcoming. She explained everything clearly and was so helpful throughout. She truly went above and beyond!

title: Caffè Nero
address: 1a Central Saint Giles Piazza, St Giles High St, London, WC2H 8AG
review_text: Staff are lovely and helpful, and there was plenty of space and nice coffee - great place to visit with small kids

title: Caffè Nero
address: 1a Central Saint Giles Piazza, St Giles High St, London, WC2H 8AG
review_text:
Three staff members were behind the counter, yet no one acknowledged me for more than two very long minutes. One person had their back turned cleaning, while the other two were chatting to each other and ignoring me.
Nice place, but the flat white was completely flat, with no layer of foam like in other cafés, and the worst brownie I’ve eaten in 16 years of living in London. It was heated until it burned and crumbled into multiple pieces (heated without asking me first, even though I actually prefer it cold), and between the grease and the crumbs it was impossible to eat. I didn’t ask for a replacement because I don’t like to cause any trouble. If you want to ruin your breakfast, this is the right place to go.

title: Caffè Nero
address: 1a Central Saint Giles Piazza, St Giles High St, London, WC2H 8AG
review_text: We ordered two hot chocolates and they filled half the cups with foam. The place was nice and busy, so i understand if the tables/seats are not really clean. They should hire more staff. I'm never coming back to this branch.

title: Caffè Nero
address: 1a Central Saint Giles Piazza, St Giles High St, London, WC2H 8AG
review_text: Great service from this cafe, especially the lady in the photo. I am pretty sure she brightens up everyone’s day in Nero with her big smiles, positive vibes and attentive service! Thank you and I will be back for the ☕️ On a side note, this outlet serves one of the best Nero coffee so far.

title: Caffè Nero
address: 1a Central Saint Giles Piazza, St Giles High St, London, WC2H 8AG
review_text: Nero always surprises with fabulous flavours and tried the spiced pecan latte and loved it...the staff at the till were very welcoming and eager to serve...Eliana and Sudha

title: Caffè Nero
address: 1a Central Saint Giles Piazza, St Giles High St, London, WC2H 8AG
review_text:
I came here with with a friend who is my especial guest.
Ordered cappuccino, chocolate and cake.
Toilet is closed as they say they need to fixe it.
The chair is totally broken and there is a huge amount of dust and rubbish under the chair cushion.

The coffee is not that perfect. Paid around £11
I couldn’t find a comfortable place to set, the table was not clean.
My guest wasn’t happy.
Staff are friendly & they are just employed.

If you through a perfect chair in London no one will pick so this chair is completely rubbish. I couldn’t understand why they keep it inside and they expect people to use it.

Many Thanks

title: Wellcome Collection Cafe
review_text:
A wonderful cafe in a beautiful, inspiring setting.

The food is delicious and the salads were imaginative. The portions were generous.

The staff are lovely - helpful and accommodating.

Already looking forward to my next visit.

title: Wellcome Collection Cafe
review_text: Wonderful cafe in the Museum. Anyone can visit even without being inside the Museum. Great selection of food and sweets. A nice relaxed Cafe with great location.

title: Wellcome Collection Cafe
review_text: Visited the Welcome Collection on a rainy Monday 30/09/2024 wishing to see an exhibition or two only to discover they're always closed on Mondays! The staff on duty were really lovely and so helpful 🌟 Instead we went to the cafe for hot drinks and to the shop. The shop's great and always worth a visit but the cafè invariably has really slow baristas and many people were hanging around waiting for their orders.

title: Wellcome Collection Cafe
review_text:
I had the carrot cake, which was very tasty. If I can pick on something, it would be that the frosting was very sugary.

From what I could see, they only had two vegan cakes and one falafel wrap as vegan offerings.

Great museum concept, but the café is nothing special.

This might be because I'm a tourist, but £4,95 for an average sized slize of cake sounds excessive to me.

Staff didn't care much.

title: Colicci Serpentine Coffee House
review_text: I stumbled upon the Serpentine Coffee House after a morning walk in Hyde Park, and it was a gem. My oat milk Americano was fresh and delicious, and the ham and cheese sandwich was warmed up quickly. The staff were friendly, the prices reasonable, and the cozy atmosphere was perfect after the chilly outdoors. If you’re visiting Hyde Park, it’s definitely worth a stop!

title: Colicci Serpentine Coffee House
review_text:
This is a relatively small yet cozy café in Hyde Park, ideal if you’re in the area and don’t mind a few hungry birds eyeing your sandwich.

Despite its compact size and focus on takeaway, the café offers a reasonably wide selection. Prices are as expected for one of the park’s few proper dining spots—on the higher side.

Seating options include both indoor and outdoor areas, with lovely views of the lake. The service is satisfactory, and the overall ambiance is quite pleasant.

My recommendation: stick to drinks and snacks, as the food isn’t particularly memorable. Still, it’s worth visiting for a slow, relaxed morning in the park.

title: Colicci Serpentine Coffee House
review_text:
The amount of plastic bottled water being sold at concessions within Hyde Park was astounding to me.

Stewardship of a major urban park like this requires mindful enough leadership that would not approve such reckless sale and consumption of the very thing that pollutes parks like this one: plastic.

By 2050, there will be more plastic in the ocean than fish, and over 95% of the plastic we recycled does not get recycled. Knowing that that is true, the Royal Parks and all of the concessions within it need to make a choice to stop the sale of this nasty product in the name of a better vision for what green spaces once were, and what they can be.

Because to be sure, we did not used to live this way. And we cannot continue to live this way. Plastics are killing us. Thank you for considering my feedback, and for seeking to be a true leader in the field of urban parks and parks concessions.

title: Colicci Serpentine Coffee House
review_text:
I came here to work on my laptop, and it was one of my best home offices.

It's really healthy to work at the outside tables with the sun, air, water and some birds. Really cozy.

The coffee is good, specialty and so is the food. The attention of the service is really lovely.

title: Colicci Serpentine Coffee House
review_text: Very limited beverage range. The only sugar free drink is Diet Coke selling £2.8 for a can. Outside lake is quite beautiful.

title: Colicci Serpentine Coffee House
review_text: Just a great spot in Hyde Park with an awesome fountain view, especially in the morning, it’s seriously stunning. The staff are super friendly, the food tastes alright, and it’s priced way better than you’d expect for the quality. Perfect place to chill, whether you’re on your own or with friends and family.

title: Colicci Serpentine Coffee House
review_text:
I visited the Serpentine Coffee House in Kensington Park with friends today. It was a very windy and cold day, and we saw the café on our way to Kensington Palace, so we decided to stop by. However, from the moment we arrived, we had a disappointing experience.

There were not enough seats inside, and the three staff members glanced at us once before continuing their conversation amongst themselves. When we went to place our order, we asked for two hot chocolates, a cappuccino, and an Americano. The staff member responded curtly, asking if we wanted two cappuccinos and an Americano. When I corrected the order, they rudely insisted that I had ordered it incorrectly. Wanting to avoid a scene in front of my friends, I just smiled and placed the order.

While I don't expect exceptional friendliness when being served, there's no need for such a cynical attitude. This visit was very unpleasant, and I have no intention of returning. One star.

Additionally, the prices are not cheap. If there are any sadists who enjoy paying to be treated rudely, this café will provide them with the perfect experience.

title: Colicci Serpentine Coffee House
review_text: I had only a cup of hot chocolate which is good. The taste was more sweet than I expected. The service was fast and okay. There are other stuffs I haven't try yet like gelato, soft ice cream, waffles, cakes etc.

title: Sunsnacks Cafe
review_text:
Great place for good quality fairly priced food
Well presented , good quality produce nicely cooked. Friendly and Happy Staff
Lots of great choices 18  standard brunch / breakfast offers plus a variety of home cooked lunches plus jacket potatoes and a great selection of sandwiches made to order.
Clean and tidy cafe located just round the corner from Marylebone main line station along the road from the seashells restaurant of Lisson Grove. Well worth a visit
10 mins walk from regent park, Baker Street , and not too far from Madamme Tussaud’s.

title: Sunsnacks Cafe
review_text: I would like to start by saying the cashier service was very rude and unhelpful. The food is mediocre at best. Found 2 hairs on my plate under the chips. Chips were wrinkly and obvious that it has been re heated. Ordered a veggie burger along with that , the veggie patty was solid in texture and definitely kept in the fridge for a while. Only thing that seemed fresh were the buns. For the price they’re charging I would expect fresh food. Premium prices for mediocre food. Haven’t seen any hygiene rating on the window. Will not be visiting again and would not recommend to anyone who appreciates food service and fresh food. 1 star would be a compliment. Owner doesn’t seem to appreciate his customers.

title: Market Cafe
review_text:
Our friend who happened to be our guide too in London suggested that we eat brunch here after browsing through the market. The food was ok, but when it was time to pay, the waitress looked irritated that each person settled their own bill.
Well, it’s better than having 8 people sit in 8 separate tables. Besides, it’s not like you’re using an abacus to add up what each one of us consumed. lol

title: Hard Rock London Rock Shop
review_text: Perfect service and wonderful hosts. I really loved this place. I visit a Hard Rock Cafe every time I go to a new city, and this one has become one of my favorites. As I learned, this is the very first Hard Rock Cafe in the world. There is an impressive memorabilia collection (including items from Jim Morrison, the Sex Pistols, and Eric Clapton, who started the memorabilia tradition). There is also a place called the Vault that you can visit like a museum. Unfortunately, I couldn’t visit it because maintenance was taking place. As a fan, it was a pleasure to visit the very first Hard Rock Cafe ever.

title: Hard Rock London Rock Shop
review_text: We visited the Hard Rock shop straight after dinning at the restaurant accross the street. This shop has the addition of a small but quite wonderful little museum (the vault). The museum includes items from various legends, and the chap we had giving us the tour was incredibly knowledgable. He got the group taking photos, and got the kids posing with various items and giving them homework to look up when they get home! A great experience / visit!

title: Hard Rock London Rock Shop
review_text:
Smaller & not many choices as a shop compared to the Picadilly one, but you come here for the Vault! This is the original HRC, and the vault quality is just legendary.
Ash is also the perfect guide for your tour there. Very knowledgeable & rocks

title: Hard Rock London Rock Shop
review_text:
Visited the place after our lunch. The staff here is the same friendly as in the restaurant.

Our guide during visiting the Vault was really passionate and shared lots of stories and interesting facts about the place, the stars and bands.

title: Hard Rock London Rock Shop
review_text: Overpriced for what it is, I think I paid £10 or something like that for a fridge magnet.. Would liked to have seen some actual rock merchandise, rather than Jessie J's outfit. But still a cool tourist spot.

title: Hard Rock London Rock Shop
review_text: This is about the vault under the shop. There is a stairway down that you can only use with a guide who was very enthusiastic and knowledgeable. The Jimi Hendrix flying V was out on lone or it would have been five stars.

title: The Park Cafe
review_text:
I am writing to make a complaint about the service my family and I received during our visit to Queens park cafe.

When we arrived, I greeted the gentleman at the counter, but he did not respond. He had his back to us while making a cigarette and was watching us through the mirror, which felt disrespectful. When he eventually turned around, he seemed angry.

As I looked at the menu, he made me feel rushed. When I asked about a Power Rangers option, his response was abrupt. Even when I chose sandwiches, he appeared unhappy to serve us. Overall, his attitude made us feel very uncomfortable, and I was disappointed with how we were treated.

title: Park Life Cafe
review_text: Today we visited the Britannia Leisure Centre, where everyone is very kind, helpful, and cheerful, and the centre is really great. I was even pleasantly surprised to find that they have a café restaurant here. There was no problem with the first coffee order, and we were served very nicely. However, things were different with the second order. Some man went to the storage room to get me a drink but then came back and handed me a pastry with his bare hands. When I asked him where the tongs were, he replied that he didn't need them because he was the 'chef.' I refused the pastry, and when I asked him about health and safety because he touch everything around he responded in a rude tone, saying 'thank you, goodbye.'

title: Cafe closed
address: 45 England's Ln, Belsize Park, London, NW3 4YD
review_text: Great breakfast place!! Very good food. Pricing reasonable, fast service. We couldn't be happier :) definitely well worth visiting. Great atmosphere and friendly fast service. We'll be coming back :)

title: Tây Đô Cafe (Vietnamese Cuisine)
review_text: Food was nice the first time I had it but then then the third time I was refused service simply because I only wanted to order egg fried rice as a takeaway. Yet they were open and claiming not to be. Quite strange.

title: Old St Burrito Cafe
review_text:
I'm on holiday in London and this cafe is right by my hotel. Toki was the sweetest person I've met while here. The food was amazing and the coffee was delicious. I will definitely go back while I'm here.

I had the scrambled egg with turkey sausage and brown pita. It was so fresh and made to order. It was a very pleasant experience. I would recommend to anyone in the area
